Lightsaber.name: Lightsaber
Lightsaber.description: An elegant weapon for a more civilized age. This energy blade can cut through nearly anything and is the signature weapon of both Jedi and Sith.

blaster pistol.name: blaster pistol
blaster pistol.description: A standard ranged energy weapon commonly used throughout the galaxy. Reliable and easy to conceal.

vibrosword.name: vibrosword
vibrosword.description: A blade weapon with an ultrasonic vibration generator that increases cutting power. Effective against most armor.

spiked bat.name: spiked bat
spiked bat.description: A crude but effective blunt weapon with metal spikes welded to its surface. Favored by thugs and enforcers.

Rocket Launcher.name: Rocket Launcher
Rocket Launcher.description: A shoulder-mounted heavy weapon that fires explosive projectiles. Devastating against vehicles and fortified positions.

thermal detonator.name: thermal detonator
thermal detonator.description: A powerful spherical explosive device that creates a devastating blast radius. Handle with extreme caution.

first aid kit.name: first aid kit
first aid kit.description: A portable medical kit containing bandages, bacta patches, and basic medical supplies for treating injuries in the field.

animal treats.name: animal treats
animal treats.description: Specially formulated food pellets designed to calm and attract various animal species across the galaxy.

forgery kit.name: forgery kit
forgery kit.description: A collection of specialized tools, inks, and materials for creating convincing counterfeit documents and credentials.

scalpel.name: scalpel
scalpel.description: A precision surgical blade used for delicate medical procedures.

droid repair kit.name: droid repair kit
droid repair kit.description: A specialized toolkit containing components and tools specifically designed for repairing and maintaining droids.

navigation computer datapad.name: navigation computer datapad
navigation computer datapad.description: A portable device containing star charts, hyperspace route calculations, and navigation software.

engineer's toolkit.name: engineer's toolkit
engineer's toolkit.description: A comprehensive set of tools for constructing, repairing, and modifying mechanical and technological systems.

credits.name: credits
credits.description: The standard currency of the galaxy, accepted across most civilized worlds.

concealed holdout blaster.name: concealed holdout blaster
concealed holdout blaster.description: A compact, easily hidden blaster pistol favored by those who need discreet protection.

hunting knife.name: hunting knife
hunting knife.description: A durable blade designed for skinning game and survival situations.

bowcaster.name: bowcaster
bowcaster.description: A Wookiee crossbow weapon that fires explosive quarrels with devastating force.

thermal imaging goggles.name: thermal imaging goggles
thermal imaging goggles.description: Enhanced vision equipment that detects heat signatures and allows vision in darkness.

meditation beads.name: meditation beads
meditation beads.description: Traditional focusing aids used during Force meditation and contemplation.

tactical datapad.name: tactical datapad
tactical datapad.description: A military-grade device for analyzing battle scenarios and coordinating strategic operations.

translator module.name: translator module
translator module.description: A device that enables real-time translation of millions of languages and dialects across the galaxy.

diplomatic credentials.name: diplomatic credentials
diplomatic credentials.description: Official documentation granting diplomatic immunity and access to government facilities.

arc welder attachment.name: arc welder attachment
arc welder attachment.description: A retractable welding tool used for repairs and emergency modifications to mechanical systems.

computer interface arm.name: computer interface arm
computer interface arm.description: A specialized appendage allowing direct connection to computer systems and data ports.

integrated blaster rifle.name: integrated blaster rifle
integrated blaster rifle.description: A built-in blaster weapon system permanently mounted to a droid's chassis.

medical scanner.name: medical scanner
medical scanner.description: A handheld diagnostic device that analyzes biological functions and detects injuries or illnesses.

surgical tools.name: surgical tools
surgical tools.description: A comprehensive set of precision instruments for performing complex medical procedures.

E-5 blaster rifle.name: E-5 blaster rifle
E-5 blaster rifle.description: A standard-issue blaster rifle used by B1 battle droids throughout the Separatist army. Reliable but somewhat inaccurate.

poison dart launcher.name: poison dart launcher
poison dart launcher.description: A concealed wrist-mounted weapon that fires toxic darts capable of incapacitating or killing targets silently.

holographic disguise matrix.name: holographic disguise matrix
holographic disguise matrix.description: An advanced device that projects a holographic disguise over the wearer, allowing them to impersonate other individuals.

integrated dual wrist blasters.name: integrated dual wrist blasters
integrated dual wrist blasters.description: Built-in blaster weapons mounted in both wrists of a B2 super battle droid, providing constant firepower.

twin repeating blaster cannons.name: twin repeating blaster cannons
twin repeating blaster cannons.description: Powerful dual blaster cannons integrated into a droideka's frame, capable of sustained heavy fire.

personal deflector shield.name: personal deflector shield
personal deflector shield.description: An energy shield generator that creates a protective barrier around the user, deflecting blaster fire and physical attacks.

DC-15S blaster carbine.name: DC-15S blaster carbine
DC-15S blaster carbine.description: A compact blaster rifle issued to clone troopers, offering excellent accuracy and stopping power in a portable package.

underwater breathing apparatus.name: underwater breathing apparatus
underwater breathing apparatus.description: A portable device that allows non-aquatic species to breathe underwater for extended periods.

antiox breath mask.name: antiox breath mask
antiox breath mask.description: A specialized mask that filters oxygen-rich atmospheres for species that require different atmospheric compositions.

clone trooper armor.name: clone trooper armor
clone trooper armor.description: Standard issue white plastoid armor worn by Republic clone troopers, offering solid protection against blaster fire.

modified light freighter.name: modified light freighter
modified light freighter.description: A small cargo vessel with enhanced engines and hidden compartments, perfect for smuggling operations.

smuggler's blaster pistol.name: smuggler's blaster pistol
smuggler's blaster pistol.description: A reliable blaster pistol modified for quick draw and concealment.

hidden cargo compartments.name: hidden cargo compartments
hidden cargo compartments.description: Concealed storage spaces built into a ship's hull to hide contraband from customs inspections.

forged credentials.name: forged credentials
forged credentials.description: Fake identification documents and permits that appear authentic under casual inspection.

comlink scrambler.name: comlink scrambler
comlink scrambler.description: A device that encrypts communications and prevents eavesdropping on transmissions.

quickdraw blaster pistols.name: quickdraw blaster pistols
quickdraw blaster pistols.description: A matched pair of blaster pistols designed for rapid firing and fast-draw techniques.

gunslinger's duster coat.name: gunslinger's duster coat
gunslinger's duster coat.description: A long coat made of durable fabric with hidden armor plating in vital areas.

Republic credits.name: Republic credits
Republic credits.description: The standard currency of the Galactic Republic, widely accepted across civilized space.

botanical specimens kit.name: botanical specimens kit
botanical specimens kit.description: A collection of containers and preservation tools for gathering and studying plant life from various worlds.

Mandalorian beskar armor.name: Mandalorian beskar armor
Mandalorian beskar armor.description: Sacred armor forged from beskar iron, passed down through Mandalorian generations and nearly impervious to damage.

Mandalorian helmet.name: Mandalorian helmet
Mandalorian helmet.description: A T-visored helmet with integrated HUD, environmental seals, and enhanced vision modes.

beskar vambraces.name: beskar vambraces
beskar vambraces.description: Forearm armor plates that often house integrated weapons and tools.

jetpack.name: jetpack
jetpack.description: A rocket pack allowing short-duration flight and aerial maneuverability.

Mandalorian blaster pistol.name: Mandalorian blaster pistol
Mandalorian blaster pistol.description: A customized blaster pistol built to Mandalorian specifications for reliability and power.

vibroblade.name: vibroblade
vibroblade.description: A combat knife with an ultrasonic vibration generator that increases its cutting power.

quick-release holster rig.name: quick-release holster rig
quick-release holster rig.description: A specialized holster system designed for lightning-fast weapon draws.

Star Wars Galaxy.name: Star Wars Galaxy
Star Wars Galaxy.basicInfo: A vast galaxy containing billions of stars and countless inhabited worlds, connected by hyperspace lanes that enable faster-than-light travel. The galaxy is divided into distinct regions: the Core Worlds (including the capital planet Coruscant), the Inner Rim, the Expansion Region, the Mid Rim, the Outer Rim Territories, and the Unknown Regions. Currently embroiled in the Clone Wars, the galaxy faces its greatest crisis in a millennium as the Galactic Republic battles the Confederacy of Independent Systems. Ancient Force traditions, advanced technology, and diverse species define this realm where politics, war, and destiny collide. The mystical energy field known as the Force permeates all living things, though few can harness its power.

Core Worlds.name: Core Worlds
Core Worlds.basicInfo: The Core Worlds represent the ancient heart of galactic civilization, containing some of the oldest, wealthiest, and most influential planets in the galaxy. Dominated by the ecumenopolis of Coruscant—home to the Galactic Senate, Jedi Temple, and trillions of inhabitants—this region is the political, economic, and cultural center of the Republic. Other major worlds include Corellia (renowned for its shipyards), Alderaan (a peaceful world of culture and diplomacy), Chandrila, and Kuat (famous for its orbital drive yards). The Core Worlds enjoy the highest standard of living, most advanced infrastructure, and strongest Republic military presence. During the Clone Wars, these worlds remain largely secure from Separatist attack, though political intrigue and espionage threaten from within. Hyperspace lanes are densely interconnected here, making travel swift and efficient. The region's immense wealth and power make it both the prize and staging ground of the galactic conflict.

Colonies - North Arc.name: Colonies - North Arc
Colonies - North Arc.basicInfo: The northern stretch of the Colonies region—densely settled, industrialized worlds sitting just beyond the Core. Trade routes from the Mid Rim feed into towering megacities and shipyards that help power the Republic war machine.

Colonies - South Arc.name: Colonies - South Arc
Colonies - South Arc.basicInfo: The southern Colonies, a mix of long-settled industrial hubs and logistics worlds that connect the Core to the Manufacturing Mid Rim. Heavily patrolled and dotted with depots, it’s where fleets refuel and armies stage before heading outward.

Inner Rim - North Reach.name: Inner Rim - North Reach
Inner Rim - North Reach.basicInfo: A prosperous band of Inner Rim worlds lying above the Core on galactic charts. These systems balance Core-level comfort with Mid Rim frontier opportunities, making them magnets for merchants and diplomats.

Inner Rim - South Reach.name: Inner Rim - South Reach
Inner Rim - South Reach.basicInfo: The southern Inner Rim, containing agro-worlds, noble estates, and heavily fortified bastion systems. Long supply lines to the Outer Rim move through here, so both Republic and Separatists constantly probe its defenses.

Inner Rim - East Reach.name: Inner Rim - East Reach
Inner Rim - East Reach.basicInfo: An Inner Rim corridor leading toward Hutt Space and the Eastern Mid Rim. Legitimate trade shares space with spice runners and smugglers trying to thread the needle between Republic customs and Hutt influence.

Inner Rim - West Reach.name: Inner Rim - West Reach
Inner Rim - West Reach.basicInfo: The western bend of the Inner Rim, a quieter stretch of space where local sector governments hold more sway than Coruscant’s bureaucracy. Ideal territory for Jedi mediators and wandering troubleshooters.

Expansion Region - North Corridor.name: Expansion Region - North Corridor
Expansion Region - North Corridor.basicInfo: A band of worlds above the Core where early Republic expansion carved out rich mining and manufacturing sectors. Many systems here are old colonial powers in their own right, exploiting Mid Rim neighbors for resources.

Expansion Region - South Corridor.name: Expansion Region - South Corridor
Expansion Region - South Corridor.basicInfo: The southern Expansion Region, dense with refineries and logistics hubs that keep the war effort supplied. Labor disputes and Separatist agitators make it a constant security headache.

Mid Rim - Northwest Quadrant.name: Mid Rim - Northwest Quadrant
Mid Rim - Northwest Quadrant.basicInfo: Forest worlds, trading hubs, and contested sectors in the upper-left Mid Rim. Not as neglected as the Outer Rim, but far enough from Coruscant that local powers and crime syndicates thrive in the gaps.

Mid Rim - Northeast Quadrant.name: Mid Rim - Northeast Quadrant
Mid Rim - Northeast Quadrant.basicInfo: Bustling trade routes, neutral systems, and politically important worlds that sit between the Expansion Region and Hutt Space. Many of the Clone Wars’ early diplomatic flashpoints happen here.

Mid Rim - Southwest Quadrant.name: Mid Rim - Southwest Quadrant
Mid Rim - Southwest Quadrant.basicInfo: A relatively underdeveloped wedge of the Mid Rim, full of sparsely populated systems and frontier colonies. Smugglers and mercenaries use these backwaters to stage runs into richer regions.

Mid Rim - Southeast Quadrant.name: Mid Rim - Southeast Quadrant
Mid Rim - Southeast Quadrant.basicInfo: Strategic Mid Rim sectors bordering the Outer Rim and key trade routes. Agricultural planets and culture-rich worlds here often become battlefields when the Clone Wars spill outward.

Outer Rim - Northern Reach.name: Outer Rim - Northern Reach
Outer Rim - Northern Reach.basicInfo: The upper sweep of the Outer Rim, where icy worlds, hidden Jedi outposts, and remote mining colonies cling to survival. Sparse traffic and brutal conditions make it perfect for secret weapons testing or exile.

Outer Rim - Eastern Loop.name: Outer Rim - Eastern Loop
Outer Rim - Eastern Loop.basicInfo: The right-hand arc of the Outer Rim, dense with Separatist-aligned worlds, Hutt-adjacent systems, and infamous battlefields. This is where many of the Clone Wars’ most iconic engagements take place.

Outer Rim - Southern Reach.name: Outer Rim - Southern Reach
Outer Rim - Southern Reach.basicInfo: A harsh lower band of the Outer Rim, home to lava worlds, sinkhole planets, and jungle death-traps. Strategic hyperspace routes run through here, but most civilized travelers avoid it unless the war forces their hand.

Hutt Space.name: Hutt Space
Hutt Space.basicInfo: A sprawling criminal dominion overlapping the Mid and Outer Rim on the eastern side of the galaxy. Technically outside Republic control, it’s ruled by powerful Hutt kajidics who tax trade, run spice routes, and play both sides of the Clone Wars.

Mandalorian Space.name: Mandalorian Space
Mandalorian Space.basicInfo: The cluster of systems surrounding Mandalore and its protectorates, technically in the Outer Rim near the Mid Rim border. Once a fearsome star-spanning empire, it’s now a patchwork of pacifist regimes, warrior clans, and neutral worlds caught between Republic and Separatists.

Corporate Sector.name: Corporate Sector
Corporate Sector.basicInfo: A semi-autonomous eastern region granted to powerful corporations in exchange for loyalty and taxes. Mega-corps run entire star systems like company towns, with their own security fleets and laws that prioritize profit over sentient rights.

Wild Space.name: Wild Space
Wild Space.basicInfo: Sparse, poorly charted systems beyond the formal edge of the Outer Rim. Hyperspace routes are incomplete or secret, and local civilizations develop with minimal Republic contact. Perfect ground for hidden bases, exile worlds, and strange anomalies.

Unknown Regions.name: Unknown Regions
Unknown Regions.basicInfo: The farthest, most mysterious fringe of the galaxy: dense with dark matter clouds, hyperspace knots, and uncharted star clusters. Few reliable maps exist, and most expeditions never return. Rumors speak of hidden empires and ancient powers lurking here.

Byss.name: Byss
Byss.basicInfo: A remote, heavily shielded Deep Core world cloaked in strange gravitational anomalies and intense dark side saturation. Access is tightly controlled, and hyperspace approaches are perilous.

Byssian Citadel Zone.description: The primary fortified urban complex, a cluster of stark towers and bunkers housing government, laboratories, and defensive systems.

Shadowed Defense Grid.description: Orbital and ground-based weapon platforms that guard the planet, controlled from hardened command bunkers.

Deep Core Approach Lanes.description: The only semi-stable hyperspace corridors leading to Byss, littered with warning buoys and the wreckage of failed jumps.

Yavin IV.name: Yavin IV
Yavin IV.basicInfo: A humid jungle moon orbiting a gas giant, layered with ancient Massassi temples and forgotten ruins. Quiet on most charts, but heavy with old Force history and the kind of isolation smugglers love.

Great Temple Ruins.description: Cyclopean stone terraces and collapsed spires swallowed by vines, still humming with strange presence at night.

Jungle Riverways.description: Wide, muddy rivers cutting through dense canopy—good for stealth travel, bad for not getting eaten.

Hidden Landing Clearings.description: Camouflaged clearings used by offworlders who prefer not to file flight plans.

Christophsis.name: Christophsis
Christophsis.basicInfo: A mineral-rich world of glittering crystal cities and razor-edged geologic formations. Valuable, heavily contested, and easy to turn into a siege nightmare.

Crystal Metropolis Spires.description: Towering crystal-and-alloy city-structures that refract sunlight into blinding sheets across the streets.

Subsurface Crystal Mines.description: Excavation tunnels and hollowed caverns where raw crystal is cut, shipped, and fought over.

Orbital Defense Lattice.description: A tight ring of platforms and batteries built to protect the crystal wealth—and to trap invaders in a killbox.

Umbara.name: Umbara
Umbara.basicInfo: A shadowed Inner Rim world of perpetual gloom, bioluminescent flora, and brutal terrain. Everything feels like it’s watching you, because it probably is.

Twilight Basin Wilds.description: Dense, glowing forests and swampy basins where visibility lies and predators don’t.

Umbaran Capital Enclave.description: Dark, sleek architecture rising from mist—government cores, military command, and tech vaults.

Grav-Rail Tunnels.description: High-speed subterranean routes built for rapid deployment and secret logistics.

Toydaria.name: Toydaria
Toydaria.basicInfo: A swampy, river-laced world of floating bazaars and stubborn neutrality. Toydarians play hardball with diplomacy—polite smiles, iron spines.

Floating Market Platforms.description: Interlocked pontoons packed with merchants, bargaining pits, and river traffic control.

River-Delta Towns.description: Stilt-houses and workshops spread through mangroves, connected by boat lanes and rope bridges.

Neutral Trade Embassy Row.description: A polished strip of consulates and negotiation halls where both sides pretend they’re not desperate.

Malastare.name: Malastare
Malastare.basicInfo: A smoky, resource-rich Mid Rim world known for heavy industry, fuel refining, and long stretches of harsh badlands. Prosperous if you’re on the right side of the contracts.

Dug Refinery Belt.description: Industrial complexes processing volatile resources into export-grade fuel and materials.

Badlands Run Tracks.description: Wide, dangerous stretches used for high-speed runs, trials, and the occasional very illegal race.

Miner Hab-Quarters.description: Worker zones and company towns built around extraction sites—rowdy, tough, and always under surveillance.

Korriban.name: Korriban
Korriban.basicInfo: A dead, dust-choked Outer Rim world of Sith tombs and ancient wars. Navigation is a pain, the atmosphere is hostile, and the history feels like a curse you can trip over.

Valley of the Dark Lords.description: A canyon of monumental tomb facades and collapsed temples, carved into red stone like old teeth.

Sith Burial Catacombs.description: Subterranean tomb networks filled with traps, sealed chambers, and lingering Force echoes.

Storm-Swept Flats.description: Open wasteland where abrasive winds strip metal and bone alike—an ugly approach to an uglier place.

Druckenwell.name: Druckenwell
Druckenwell.basicInfo: An industrial Colonies world dominated by ship hull fabrication plants, orbital yards, and smoggy factory cities that feed the Core with freighters and bulk transports.

Druckenwell Prime Yards.description: Massive orbital construction grids where civilian transports and light warships are assembled around the clock.

Industrial Hab-Stacks.description: Towering blocks of worker housing built into the sides of factory canyons, lit by neon and furnace glow.

Logistics Superports.description: Groundside and orbital superports where completed hulls are crewed, fueled, and dispatched to the Core.

Taanab.name: Taanab
Taanab.basicInfo: A fertile agricultural world of rolling plains and small towns, famous for its independent militias and large orbital trade fairs.

Panna City.description: Principal riverside settlement surrounded by fields and grain towers, hosting seasonal trade festivals.

Outer Farm Belts.description: Extensive croplands and ranches protected by local militias and sensor nets.

Orbital Fairgrounds.description: Temporary docking platforms and market stations that see heavy traffic during Taanab’s famous trade fairs.

Svivren.name: Svivren
Svivren.basicInfo: A lightly populated Colonies world of rocky mesas and deep canyons, home to reclusive settlers and a handful of monasteries.

Canyon Rim Settlements.description: Small towns perched along canyon edges, linked by bridges and lift platforms.

Svivren Monastery Heights.description: Remote cliffside monasteries where scholars and hermits study in relative isolation.

Deserted Lowlands.description: Sparse valley floors used for mining, hidden camps, and illicit dealings far from official eyes.

Gizer.name: Gizer
Gizer.basicInfo: A storm-wracked Inner Rim ocean world whose floating platforms harvest energy from colossal lightning storms and tidal surges.

Storm-Harvest Platforms.description: Linked generator platforms anchored in turbulent seas, capturing lightning and wave energy.

Platform Hab-Clusters.description: Living and commercial districts suspended above the waves, constantly buffeted by storms.

Weather Control Spires.description: Tall control towers monitoring storm patterns and redirecting excess energy into storage arrays.

Delaya.name: Delaya
Delaya.basicInfo: A cultured Inner Rim world tied historically to Alderaan, known for universities, diplomatic retreats, and lakeside cities.

Delayan Capitol.description: Elegant government and academic district overlooking a broad inland sea.

Lakeside Promenades.description: Walkways, villas, and embassies lining the water’s edge, ideal for quiet negotiations.

University Terraces.description: Stepped academic complexes and research centers attracting scholars from the Inner Rim.

Saarai.name: Saarai
Saarai.basicInfo: A mysterious Inner Rim world shrouded in permanent fog, with dense jungles and floating magnetic islands rich in rare ores.

Fog Jungle Basin.description: Ground-level jungles of twisted trees and thick mists hiding predators and ruins.

Magnetic Island Mines.description: Floating landmasses tethered by cables, studded with mining rigs harvesting exotic metals.

Hidden Research Outposts.description: Secretive labs and bunkers studying Saarai’s strange magnetic phenomena.

Gizer IV.name: Gizer IV
Gizer IV.basicInfo: A mountainous Inner Rim world marked by strong gravity fluctuations and deep mining stations carved into steep cliffs.

High Ridge Settlements.description: Towns clinging to mountain ridges with reinforced structures built to survive sudden gravity shifts.

Grav-Drift Mines.description: Deep mining levels where fluctuating gravity fields complicate extraction—and combat.

Transit Lift Shafts.description: Massive elevator systems linking surface towns to underground facilities and landing bays.

Vulpterra.name: Vulpterra
Vulpterra.basicInfo: A harsh desert Inner Rim world of rocky badlands and razor-backed dunes, famed for its trackers and survivalists.

Buried Caravan Hub.description: Half-subterranean trade settlement offering supplies and work to hunters and guides.

Dune Expanse.description: Open desert used for long-haul caravans and training runs for elite scouts.

Canyon Ambush Trails.description: Network of narrow canyons favored by raiders, bounty hunters, and local trackers.

Kirrath Prime.name: Kirrath Prime
Kirrath Prime.basicInfo: A heavily urbanized Inner Rim world wrapped by a near-continuous equatorial ring-city that handles transit and trade.

Equatorial Ring City.description: Megacity belt of towers and starports circling the world’s equator.

Old Polar Districts.description: Older cities at the poles, now overshadowed by the ring but still politically influential.

Freight Tether Arrays.description: Elevator systems and tethers linking orbital depots to surface distribution hubs.

Reecee.name: Reecee
Reecee.basicInfo: An Inner Rim repair and refit world whose shipyards specialize in restoring battered warships and freighters.

Reecee Repair Grid.description: Orbital rings and docks devoted to hull repairs, refits, and emergency overhauls.

Surface Tech Towns.description: Cities built around fabrication plants and training centers for engineers and mechanics.

Graveyard Orbit.description: Cloud of derelict hulks used for parts salvage and training runs.

Svivren II.name: Svivren II
Svivren II.basicInfo: A nearby system mate to Svivren, this Inner Rim world is dominated by mountain ranges and glacial valleys.

High Valley Settlements.description: Villages nestled in sheltered valleys between snowy peaks.

Glacial Lakes.description: Cold, clear lakes supplying water and fish to surrounding communities.

Mountain Pass Routes.description: Treacherous passes used by traders and smugglers moving between regions.

Zygerria.name: Zygerria
Zygerria.basicInfo: An Inner Rim biotech monarchy world whose nobles fund cutting-edge genetic and medical research for profit and prestige.

Royal Biotech Citadel.description: Palace complex fused with high-security laboratories and medical towers.

Research Sector Sprawl.description: Grid of specialized labs and corporate compounds competing for breakthroughs.

Merchant-Clinic District.description: Public front of clinics and treatment centers offering advanced (and expensive) care.

Lorahna.name: Lorahna
Lorahna.basicInfo: A serene forest-and-lake Inner Rim world known for its pharmaceuticals, retreats, and quiet resort towns.

Lorahna Lakeside City.description: Main city spread along a wide lake, combining medical centers with upscale resorts.

Forest Retreat Zones.description: Healing lodges and spa enclaves built within carefully preserved woodland.

Pharma Complexes.description: Biotech and pharmaceutical plants producing high-grade medicines for the Core.

Atravis.name: Atravis
Atravis.basicInfo: A resource-rich Inner Rim system built around hollowed asteroids and massive artificial habitats.

Atravis Habitat Station.description: Central O’Neill-style cylinder station housing most of the system’s population.

Asteroid Mining Fields.description: Clusters of mined-out rocks and active dig-sites generating ore and salvage.

Outer Dock Web.description: Network of docks and repair bays linking traffic between the fields and main habitat.

Ralltiir.name: Ralltiir
Ralltiir.basicInfo: An Inner Rim banking and trade world where corporate towers and financial institutions dominate the skyline.

Ralltiir Finance Core.description: Dense cluster of skyscrapers housing major banks, brokerages, and trade authorities.

Transit Spoke Terminals.description: Starport and maglev hubs funneling offworld visitors straight into the financial district.

Old Merchant Quarter.description: Historic market streets now overshadowed by towering financial complexes.

Boudolayz.name: Boudolayz
Boudolayz.basicInfo: A hot, steam-jungle Inner Rim world with geyser fields, sulfur pools, and dense biodiversity.

Geyser Basin Settlements.description: Towns perched on stable ground between violently active geyser fields.

Steam Jungle Belts.description: Thick vegetation and constant mist, home to aggressive fauna and rare plants.

Survey Outposts.description: Scientific and corporate stations cataloguing resources and testing equipment.

Haruun.name: Haruun
Haruun.basicInfo: A dry-mesa Inner Rim world of red rock plateaus, narrow canyons, and nomadic warrior clans.

Mesa City Encampment.description: Central settlement built atop a broad mesa, blending stonework with tents and landing pads.

Canyon War Trails.description: Network of winding canyons used for ambushes, duels, and clan skirmishes.

Nomad Oasis Fields.description: Sparse water sources and farmland controlled by roaming clans and guarded fiercely.

Frego.name: Frego
Frego.basicInfo: An Inner Rim culture world of ornate cities, night-lit canals, and a reputation for art, music, and subtle intrigue.

Canal City Promenades.description: Central urban district of bridges, waterways, and performance plazas.

Opera and Gallery Quarter.description: Cluster of grand theaters, galleries, and salons where deals are struck over culture and wine.

Harbor Starport.description: Busy starport along the coastline, bringing tourists, merchants, and spies alike.

Abregado-Rae.name: Abregado-Rae
Abregado-Rae.basicInfo: A Mid Rim trade and transit world at a key hyperspace junction, balancing legitimate commerce and smuggling.

Abregado Starport Ring.description: Orbital docks and waystations handling ships traveling the main lanes.

City-Below Districts.description: Dense surface city sectors full of warehouses, cantinas, and cheap lodgings.

Spacer Market Quarter.description: Bazaar-like district where cargo, information, and favors are bought and sold.

Jabiim.name: Jabiim
Jabiim.basicInfo: A mud-choked, stormy Mid Rim world notorious for trench warfare and endless rain-slick battlefields.

Mudfront Trenches.description: Endless lines of trenches and bunkers dug into saturated soil.

Command Ridge Encampments.description: Elevated positions housing command posts and artillery.

Forward Supply Pits.description: Half-sunken depots and staging areas constantly threatened by floods and bombardment.

Polis Massa.name: Polis Massa
Polis Massa.basicInfo: A remote asteroid world with medical research facilities built into hollowed rocks and linked stations.

Central Medical Complex.description: Primary treatment and research hub carved into a large asteroid.

Habitat Ring Modules.description: Linked residential and support sections for staff and patients.

Outer Excavation Sites.description: Dig-sites and labs exploring ancient remains within neighboring asteroids.

Dathomir.name: Dathomir
Dathomir.basicInfo: A savage Mid Rim wasteland of jagged red rock, ash-choked canyons, and twisted dead forests, home to dangerous fauna, Force-wielding clans, and roaming rancors.

Rancor Valley.description: Broad canyon where rancors roam and hunt among shattered stone pillars and bone-strewn gullies.

Clan Village Enclaves.description: Hidden settlements of local Force-sensitive clans carved into cliff faces with strict traditions and blood-red banners.

Swamp Edges.description: Murky transition zones crawling with predators and strange plant life.

Ash Wastes.description: Desolate expanses of volcanic ash and cracked earth crawling with predators and strange, corrupted plant life.

Siskeen.name: Siskeen
Siskeen.basicInfo: A Mid Rim frontier agriworld of dusty plains and hardened communities accustomed to raids.

Siskeen Farmsteads.description: Scattered farms fortified with walls, turrets, and local militia barracks.

Dust Road Crossings.description: Intersections of trade routes and ambush points used by raiders and defenders alike.

Siskeen Starfield Port.description: Primary starport town serving traders, mercenaries, and local defense forces.

Gand.name: Gand
Gand.basicInfo: A Mid Rim ammonia-atmosphere world of dense fog banks and city-bubbles, home to the insectoid Gand species.

Gand Enclosure Cities.description: Domed cities where breathable atmospheres and commerce are carefully regulated.

Fog Wastes.description: Thickly shrouded surface zones where visibility is minimal and tracking is an art.

Hunter Guild Halls.description: Enclaves housing Gand Findsmen and other tracker societies.

Rori.name: Rori
Rori.basicInfo: A swamp-and-forest moon of Naboo with rougher settlements, wildlife reserves, and frontier outposts.

Narmle Outpost Town.description: Primary settlement balancing trade, frontier work, and minor tourism.

Swamp Reserve Zones.description: Protected wetlands containing rare species and hidden smuggler routes.

Forest Patrol Trails.description: Paths used by scouts and rangers to monitor wildlife and illegal activity.

Kerenova.name: Kerenova
Kerenova.basicInfo: A warm Mid Rim world of tropical archipelagos, racing circuits, and upscale island resorts.

Kerenova Grand Circuit.description: High-speed racing track winding across bridges and coastal roads.

Resort Island Cluster.description: Chain of luxury islands boasting beaches, casinos, and private docks.

Harbor Starport.description: Main offworld access point and cargo hub supporting tourism and racing events.

Maraan.name: Maraan
Maraan.basicInfo: A tranquil Mid Rim spiritual world of mountain temples, meditation enclaves, and quiet valley towns.

High Temple Terraces.description: Linked temple complexes built along mountain ridges overlooking cloud seas.

Valley Pilgrim Roads.description: Stone paths winding through villages that cater to pilgrims and scholars.

Meditation Lake Retreats.description: Remote lodges and sanctuaries on still lakes used for study and contemplation.

Carlac.name: Carlac
Carlac.basicInfo: A frigid Outer Rim world of snowfields and icy forests with isolated villages and old aristocratic estates.

Snowbound Villages.description: Small settlements clustered around geothermal vents and trade posts.

Frozen Estate Grounds.description: Old noble compounds partly buried in snow, used for politics and quiet deals.

Pine Forest Roads.description: Long, icy roads through dark forests patrolled by local militias.

Orto Plutonia.name: Orto Plutonia
Orto Plutonia.basicInfo: An iceball Outer Rim world of blizzards and subterranean caverns warmed by geothermal vents.

Surface Ice Plains.description: Open frozen plains regularly swept by lethal storms.

Subterranean Cavern Settlements.description: Habitations dug into caverns beneath the ice, clustered around stable heat sources.

Geothermal Vent Fields.description: Hot vents and fumaroles providing warmth, power, and dangerous terrain.

Quell.name: Quell
Quell.basicInfo: A remote, icy Outer Rim world used as a listening post and early warning station in the northern reaches.

Quell Listening Post.description: Fortified base housing scanners, communications arrays, and a small garrison.

Frozen Ridge Patrols.description: Snowy ridges where patrols watch for intruders and sensor anomalies.

Landing Field Outcrop.description: Exposed rocky plateau serving as the world’s primary landing zone.

Bonadan.name: Bonadan
Bonadan.basicInfo: A massive Corporate Sector port world of polluted skies, sprawling docks, and megacorporate towers.

Mega-Dock Strips.description: Endless rows of starship berths, cranes, and cargo canyons.

Corporate Tower District.description: Cluster of skyscrapers housing executive offices and board suites.

Contractor Slab-Habs.description: Compressed worker housing blocks with minimal amenities and heavy surveillance.

Ammuud.name: Ammuud
Ammuud.basicInfo: A Corporate Sector world ruled by entrenched family corporations who control land, resources, and law.

Family Enclave Estates.description: Walled compounds of Ammuud’s ruling corporate families.

Central Arbitration Forum.description: Neutral-appearing plaza where inter-family disputes and contracts are formally resolved.

Factory-Farm Districts.description: Expansive agro-industrial tracts producing food and bio-products.

Drearia.name: Drearia
Drearia.basicInfo: A bleak Corporate Sector mining world blasted by toxic storms and worked largely by drones and remote crews.

Automated Strip Mines.description: Scoured surface zones where remote vehicles tear up the terrain for ore.

Storm-Hardened Control Bunkers.description: Buried command and monitoring centers shielding operators from constant storms.

Refinery Sprawl.description: Linked refining complexes converting raw ore into export-ready materials.

Zonju V.name: Zonju V
Zonju V.basicInfo: A Wild Space frontier world of rocky mesas, shanty towns, and shifting clan territories.

Zonju Shanty Port.description: Haphazard starport town serving freelancers, raiders, and wanderers.

Mesa Clan Territories.description: High plateau regions controlled by different, often feuding, local clans.

Scrapyard Flats.description: Stretches of broken machinery and crashed ships repurposed by scavengers.

Rishi.name: Rishi
Rishi.basicInfo: A humid jungle Wild Space world with tall cliffs, coastal coves, and aviary life, frequented by pirates and scouts.

Rishi Cove Anchorage.description: Hidden harbor and settlement carved into sea cliffs.

Jungle Interior Trails.description: Dense jungle routes used by hunters, scouts, and smugglers avoiding sensors.

Cliff Perch Villages.description: Small communities built on ledges overlooking sea and jungle.

Murkhana.name: Murkhana
Murkhana.basicInfo: A shadowy Wild Space world of dark forests and coastal cities, often used as a base for covert intelligence operations.

Murkhana Port City.description: Main coastal city hosting trade, intelligence fronts, and secretive offices.

Forest Hinterlands.description: Thick woodland hiding safehouses and illegal facilities far from the main city.

Encrypted Operations Complex.description: Heavily secured compound coordinating covert missions throughout nearby regions.

Empress Teta.name: Empress Teta
Empress Teta.basicInfo: An ancient Deep Core system long known for its early conquests and unified rule. Its primary world is heavily urbanized and culturally sophisticated.

Cinnagar Metropolis.description: The capital megacity, packed with towering spires, old noble houses, and dense traffic lanes.

Historic War District.description: A quarter filled with preserved battle monuments and ancient fortifications now woven into the living city.

Orbital Trade Ring.description: A ring of stations and docks circling the world, coordinating merchant traffic into the Deep Core.

Coruscant.name: Coruscant
Coruscant.basicInfo: The ecumenopolis capital of the Galactic Republic, a planet-wide city that houses the Galactic Senate, the Jedi Temple, and trillions of inhabitants.

Galactic City Sprawl.description: The endless urban surface of Coruscant, with layered traffic lanes, towers, and residential districts stretching to the horizon.

Senate District.description: The political heart of the Republic, anchored by the Senate Building and surrounding governmental complexes.

Jedi Temple Precinct.description: The plateau housing the Jedi Temple, its towers, training grounds, and archives.

Underworld Levels.description: The deep levels far below the surface, crowded with crime, gangs, and forgotten infrastructure.

Alderaan.name: Alderaan
Alderaan.basicInfo: A serene Core World renowned for its natural beauty, pacifist ideals, and influential royal family.

Aldera City.description: Graceful capital city built among mountains and rivers, blending architecture with the landscape.

Castle Grounds.description: Royal palace complex and surrounding terraces where the Organa family resides and hosts foreign envoys.

Highland Preserves.description: Protected natural zones of forests, peaks, and lakes, symbolizing Alderaan’s devotion to peace and ecology.

Corellia.name: Corellia
Corellia.basicInfo: A bustling shipbuilding Core World famous for its skilled pilots, massive shipyards, and independent streak.

Coronet City.description: Industrial capital and main starport hub, packed with docks, factories, and cantinas.

Corellian Shipyards.description: Orbital and surface shipyards constructing civilian and military vessels for the Republic and private buyers.

Old Quarter Streets.description: Tight lanes and stacked buildings where smugglers, mechanics, and old families rub shoulders.

Chandrila.name: Chandrila
Chandrila.basicInfo: A verdant Core World known for its democratic values, rolling farmlands, and political activists.

Hanna City.description: Capital city built along a wide river, home to the planetary government and major civic forums.

Riverfarmlands.description: Productive agricultural belts that feed the population and export high-quality goods to other Core worlds.

Senatorial Enclave.description: District where visiting senators and political organizers meet, debate, and coordinate policy.

Kuat.name: Kuat
Kuat.basicInfo: A Core-adjacent shipbuilding powerhouse whose massive ring-shaped shipyards dominate its orbit.

Kuat City.description: Planetary capital coordinating the flow of resources, workers, and contracts for the shipyards.

Kuat Drive Yards Ring.description: Vast orbital shipyard ring where capital ships and smaller vessels are constructed around the clock.

Worker Habitats.description: Station and surface districts providing housing, services, and rough entertainment to the workforce.

Brentaal IV.name: Brentaal IV
Brentaal IV.basicInfo: A vital trade world in the Core, controlling the intersection of major hyperspace routes.

Cirracar Trade Port.description: Primary starport city handling freight, passenger traffic, and customs inspections.

Brentaal Commerce District.description: Densely packed markets and office towers where guilds and trade houses compete for contracts.

Orbital Customs Grid.description: Network of stations and buoys tracking ships moving across the Brentaal crossroads.

Cato Neimoidia.name: Cato Neimoidia
Cato Neimoidia.basicInfo: A wealthy Colonies world famed for its bridge-cities suspended between steep rock formations, serving as a financial and storage hub for Neimoidian interests.

Bridge-Cities of Zarra.description: Primary urban centers suspended along colossal stone arches, mixing luxury estates with fortified vaults.

Trade Federation Vault District.description: Heavily guarded storage complexes where valuable goods and records are kept.

Landing Platforms.description: Exposed docking pads attached to cliff faces, constantly busy with freight and diplomatic traffic.

Commenor.name: Commenor
Commenor.basicInfo: A bustling Colonies trade world and major shipping hub that links the Core to the southern Mid Rim.

Commenor Capital Sprawl.description: The main urban region around the planet’s superports, full of merchants, haulers, and taverns.

Orbital Freight Lanes.description: Heavy-traffic corridors with cargo platforms and customs ships directing constant convoys.

Dockside Markets.description: Warehouses and open-air markets near the primary ports, where deals are closed at all hours.

Fondor.name: Fondor
Fondor.basicInfo: An industrial Colonies world dominated by orbital shipyards and sprawling factories.

Fondor Shipyard Grid.description: Linked platforms and drydocks where military and civilian craft are assembled and refitted.

Industrial Quarter.description: Surface factories, smelters, and heavy-manufacturing plants feeding the shipyards.

Worker Hab-Rings.description: Orbiting living modules and service hubs for the yard workforce.

Balmorra.name: Balmorra
Balmorra.basicInfo: A Colonies world known for weapons manufacturing and armored vehicle production.

Troida Industrial City.description: Major manufacturing center where factories produce arms and armored vehicles.

Proving Grounds.description: Wide testing ranges used to run field trials on new weapons and walkers.

Freight Rail Yards.description: Heavy cargo hubs shipping finished armaments to the Republic and private buyers.

Duro.name: Duro
Duro.basicInfo: An Inner Rim world whose polluted surface is largely abandoned, with most of the population living in orbital city-stations.

Duro Orbital Cities.description: Linked habitat stations where most Duros citizens live and work above their damaged planet.

Shipwright Platforms.description: Construction and repair docks providing ships and freighters to clients across the galaxy.

Ruined Surface Zones.description: Polluted ground-level areas used for storage, secret facilities, or hazardous experiments.

Onderon.name: Onderon
Onderon.basicInfo: A jungle-covered Inner Rim world with a history of warfare and strong local monarchs.

Iziz City.description: Walled capital city with narrow streets, royal compounds, and defensive emplacements.

Jungle Outskirts.description: Dense wilds beyond Iziz, full of dangerous beasts and rebel hideouts.

Sky Ramparts.description: Outer walls and towers lining the city edge, providing views of the jungle and landing zones.

Dxun.name: Dxun
Dxun.basicInfo: Onderon’s dangerous moon, covered in dense jungles and populated by fierce wildlife and old war relics.

Jungle Surface.description: Untamed wilderness where storms roll in frequently and predators roam freely.

Ancient Ruin Sites.description: Scattered remains of old fortifications and temples from previous conflicts.

Landing Clearings.description: Roughly cleared zones used by daring pilots and covert operatives.

Serenno.name: Serenno
Serenno.basicInfo: A noble-dominated world of estates and forests, homeworld of Count Dooku and seat of powerful aristocratic families.

Carannia City.description: Primary urban center, mixing old stone architecture with modern starports.

Dooku Family Estate.description: Expansive manor house complex atop cliffs, serving as Dooku’s ancestral home.

Forest Estates.description: Wide tracts of wooded land divided among noble houses, hosting duels and secret meetings.

Nubia.name: Nubia
Nubia.basicInfo: A quiet Inner Rim world known for its advanced starship design and elegant private vessels.

Nubian Design Quarter.description: District of starship designers and foundries producing sleek, high-end craft.

Capitol City Hub.description: Main city and governmental center, overseeing trade and contracts.

Test Flight Range.description: Designated flight corridors and space above Nubia for ship trials.

Kashyyyk.name: Kashyyyk
Kashyyyk.basicInfo: A Mid Rim forest world of towering wroshyr trees, home to the Wookiees and site of major Clone Wars battles.

Kachirho Tree-City.description: A massive settlement built into the branches of enormous trees overlooking the ocean.

Wroshyr Canopy Walks.description: Bridges and platforms connecting high-level dwellings and watch posts in the upper canopy.

Beachhead Battlefields.description: Coastal zones of sand and shallow water that saw heavy fighting during the Clone Wars.

Trandosha.name: Trandosha
Trandosha.basicInfo: A harsh, forested world near Kashyyyk, home to the Trandoshan hunters and their score-obsessed culture.

Hsskor City.description: Main urban center and marketplace for slaves, trophies, and weapons.

Hunting Terrains.description: Stalked wilderness where Trandoshans hone their skills and pursue off-world prey.

Spaceport Claw.description: Rugged starport serving bounty hunters and slavers.

Bothawui.name: Bothawui
Bothawui.basicInfo: A Mid Rim world known for its information brokers, political scheming, and Bothan spy networks.

Drev'starn City.description: Capital and hub for Bothan political and intelligence operations.

Information Exchange Plaza.description: Public-facing area masking countless covert meetings and data trades.

Starport District.description: Busy arrival point where agents come and go under layers of false identities.

Naboo.name: Naboo
Naboo.basicInfo: A picturesque Mid Rim world of plains, lakes, and classical architecture, ruled by an elected monarchy.

Theed City.description: Elegant riverside capital with palaces, plazas, and the main starport.

Theed Palace Complex.description: Seat of the Naboo monarchy and government, overlooking waterfalls and the river.

Naboo Grasslands.description: Wide plains around Theed that served as battlefields against the Trade Federation.

Gungan Sacred Lake.description: Deep lake giving access to Gungan underwater cities such as Otoh Gunga.

Ilum.name: Ilum
Ilum.basicInfo: A frigid Outer Rim world rich in kyber crystals, used by the Jedi to construct their lightsabers.

Jedi Crystal Caves.description: Network of caverns filled with resonant kyber crystals, used in Jedi trials.

Snowfields of Ilum.description: Harsh frozen surface with blizzards and deep crevasses.

Hidden Landing Zone.description: Concealed approach area where Jedi ships can set down away from prying eyes.

Geonosis.name: Geonosis
Geonosis.basicInfo: A rocky Outer Rim world of hives and factories, birthplace of the Separatist droid army.

Geonosian Arena.description: Massive amphitheater used for executions and spectacles.

Droid Foundry Complexes.description: Vast automated factories producing battle droids and war machines.

Hive Spires.description: Tall organic towers that house Geonosian castes and internal tunnels.

Ryloth.name: Ryloth
Ryloth.basicInfo: A tidally locked Outer Rim world, home of the Twi'leks, with one scorched hemisphere and one frozen.

Nabat and Numa Region.description: Twi’lek settlements and farmland clinging to the narrow habitable band.

Ryloth Capital Lessu.description: City perched atop cliffs, connected by retractable bridges for defense.

Spice Caves.description: Hidden caverns used by slavers and smugglers to store ryll spice.

Tatooine.name: Tatooine
Tatooine.basicInfo: A remote desert world in the Outer Rim, dominated by scorching suns, moisture farms, and Hutt influence.

Mos Eisley Spaceport.description: Wretched hive of smugglers, cantinas, and transient traffic.

Dune Sea.description: Endless ocean of sand dunes infested with Tusken Raiders and beasts.

Lars Homestead Region.description: Cluster of moisture farms, including the homestead where Skywalkers were raised.

Jabba's Palace.description: Fortified Hutt stronghold overlooking the desert wastes.

Kessel.name: Kessel
Kessel.basicInfo: A rugged Outer Rim world famous for its spice mines and treacherous surrounding maelstrom.

Spice Mines of Kessel.description: Harsh prison-mines where workers extract valuable spice under brutal conditions.

Kessel Starport.description: Tightly controlled docking hub where shipments are loaded and guarded.

Kessel Maelstrom Edge.description: Hazardous surrounding nebula region with warped gravity and navigation hazards.

Utapau.name: Utapau
Utapau.basicInfo: A sinkhole-riddled Outer Rim world whose cities are built into deep craters, later hosting major Clone Wars battles.

Pau City.description: Primary sinkhole city with tall rock walls and layered platforms.

Surface Wind Plains.description: Barren wind-blasted surface used as landing and staging ground.

Sinkhole Networks.description: Interlinked sinkholes and subterranean passages connecting other Utapaun settlements.

Mustafar.name: Mustafar
Mustafar.basicInfo: A volcanic Outer Rim world of lava flows and industrial facilities, far from normal trade routes.

Lava Processing Facility.description: Industrial complex suspended above flowing lava used to harvest minerals and energy.

Lava River Platforms.description: Maintenance and observation platforms precariously perched over molten rivers.

Landing Pad Complex.description: Isolated landing zone accessible only by careful approach through volcanic storms.

Felucia.name: Felucia
Felucia.basicInfo: A jungle world whose bioluminescent, oversized flora and dangerous fauna make it both beautiful and deadly.

Felucian Jungle Plains.description: Dense fungal forests and towering plants where visibility is limited and ambushes are common.

Republic Outpost Ruins.description: Former military positions now overgrown or abandoned after heavy fighting.

Native Settlements.description: Small villages belonging to Felucian natives, hidden within the vibrant terrain.

Saleucami.name: Saleucami
Saleucami.basicInfo: A rugged Outer Rim world with cratered terrain and subterranean networks used during the Clone Wars.

Crater Battlefields.description: Pocked landscape bearing scars from prolonged campaigns.

Underground Tunnel Nets.description: Extensive cave systems and man-made tunnels used for troop movement.

Settlement Outskirts.description: Rural zones around small towns caught between warring sides.

Mygeeto.name: Mygeeto
Mygeeto.basicInfo: A frigid crystallized Outer Rim world with harsh storms and valuable energy crystals.

Crystal City Front.description: Urban zone built among massive crystal formations, turned into a war-torn front line.

Battle-Torn Bridges.description: Long raised causeways spanning chasms, repeatedly damaged and rebuilt.

Energy Vault Complexes.description: Facilities used to store and process Mygeeto’s unique energy crystals.

Nal Hutta.name: Nal Hutta
Nal Hutta.basicInfo: The swampy, polluted Hutt homeworld at the heart of Hutt Space.

Hutt Council City.description: Sprawling palace and business complexes where Hutt leaders convene and scheme.

Swamp Outskirts.description: Mired wetlands surrounding the main settlements, dotted with shanties and hidden labs.

Star Docks of Nal Hutta.description: Orbital and ground docking facilities handling contraband and legitimate trade alike.

Nar Shaddaa.name: Nar Shaddaa
Nar Shaddaa.basicInfo: The Smugglers’ Moon, a moon of Nal Hutta completely covered in urban sprawl and crime.

Vertical City Sectors.description: Stacked levels of streets, towers, and platforms packed with all kinds of beings.

Refugee Slums.description: Crowded and dangerous lower districts where refugees and the poor struggle to survive.

Hutt-Controlled Promenades.description: Neon-lit entertainment and business strips under direct Hutt supervision.

Sleheyron.name: Sleheyron
Sleheyron.basicInfo: A hot industrial Hutt Space world used for gladiatorial arenas and heavy manufacturing.

Arena District.description: Cluster of gladiatorial pits where fighters battle for Hutt entertainment.

Industrial Works.description: Foundries and factories producing weapons and machinery.

Slave Barracks.description: Utilitarian housing blocks holding enslaved workers and fighters.

Mandalore.name: Mandalore
Mandalore.basicInfo: The war-scarred capital world of the Mandalorians, partially glassed and rebuilt into domed cities.

Sundari Domed City.description: Central city beneath a great dome, seat of the New Mandalorian government.

Outlands Wastes.description: Desolate terrain outside the domes, dotted with old battle wreckage.

Royal Residence Sector.description: Quarter that houses leadership and key government buildings.

Concordia.name: Concordia
Concordia.basicInfo: A moon of Mandalore used as a mining colony and covert base for certain Mandalorian factions.

Mining Settlements.description: Towns built around resource extraction sites along Concordia’s surface.

Hidden Clan Enclave.description: Secluded strongholds used by militant Mandalorians.

Landing Fields.description: Rugged landing pads and hangars sunk into mesa plateaus.

Kalevala.name: Kalevala
Kalevala.basicInfo: A temperate world within Mandalorian Space that hosts noble houses and political intrigue.

Kalevala Noble Estates.description: Expansive estates belonging to Mandalorian noble families.

Provincial Capital.description: Administrative city where local governance and diplomacy occur.

Spaceport Plateau.description: Elevated starport area handling visiting envoys and clan ships.

Etti IV.name: Etti IV
Etti IV.basicInfo: The primary world of the Corporate Sector Authority, run like a massive corporate headquarters-planet.

Corporate Sector Plaza.description: Central business district where mega-corps maintain towering headquarters.

Authority Administration Hub.description: Government complex where the Corporate Sector Authority manages its territories.

Contractor Habitation Zones.description: Densely packed housing districts for mid-level employees and contractors.

Kamino.name: Kamino
Kamino.basicInfo: An isolated ocean world in Wild Space, host to advanced cloning facilities that produced the Republic’s clone army.

Tipoca City.description: Primary storm-lashed cloning city raised above endless oceans on stilts.

Cloning Facility Core.description: Sterile white corridors lined with growth chambers containing thousands of developing clones at various stages of maturation.

Training Simulation Halls.description: Combat training rooms where clone cadets practice tactics, weapons handling, and battlefield scenarios under Kaminoan supervision.

Cloning Facility Platforms.description: Interior complexes where clones are grown, trained, and conditioned.

Landing Platforms.description: Open external platforms used by visiting Jedi and Republic officers.

Storm-Swept Observation Dome.description: A massive transparisteel dome crowning the highest point of Tipoca City, where rain cascades down the curved surface and lightning illuminates the endless ocean below. Kaminoan officials and visiting dignitaries gather here for private discussions away from the sterile corridors.

Raxus Secundai.name: Raxus Secundai
Raxus Secundai.basicInfo: The political capital of the Confederacy of Independent Systems, a world of government complexes, Separatist propaganda monuments, and heavily fortified districts.

Separatist Parliament.description: Imposing government complex where the Separatist Council convenes and Count Dooku addresses planetary representatives.

Fortified Diplomatic Quarter.description: Secured district housing embassies and corporate offices of Trade Federation, Banking Clan, and other Separatist allies.

Dagobah.name: Dagobah
Dagobah.basicInfo: A remote Wild Space swamp world shrouded in mist and teeming with life, strong in the Force and largely uncharted.

Swamp Wilderness.description: Dense foggy wetlands filled with twisted trees, murky water, and dangerous creatures.

Dark Side Cave.description: A cave strong with the dark side of the Force, used by Jedi for trials and meditation.

Ahch-To.name: Ahch-To
Ahch-To.basicInfo: An oceanic Unknown Regions world of rocky islands and ancient Jedi temples, one of the earliest sites of the Jedi Order.

Temple Island.description: Rocky island topped with ancient stone structures and meditation platforms overlooking stormy seas.

Coastal Cliffs.description: Steep cliffs battered by waves where Force-sensitive monks once trained in isolation.

Concord Dawn.name: Concord Dawn
Concord Dawn.basicInfo: A harsh agricultural world in Mandalorian Space known for its tough farming communities and warrior traditions.

Farming Settlements.description: Scattered homesteads and small towns where Mandalorian farmers work the difficult soil.

Training Grounds.description: Open fields and combat ranges where young Mandalorians learn warrior skills alongside farming.

Yavin.name: Yavin
Yavin.basicInfo: A massive orange gas giant in the Outer Rim, orbited by numerous moons including the jungle-covered Yavin IV. The system's remote location and difficult navigation make it ideal for hidden bases.

Upper Atmosphere Stations.description: Floating research platforms skimming the gas giant's upper cloud layers, harvesting gases and monitoring the moons.

Orbital Transit Lanes.description: Navigational corridors threading between Yavin's many moons, requiring careful piloting to avoid gravitational interference.

Yavin 8.name: Yavin 8
Yavin 8.basicInfo: A smaller jungle moon of Yavin with dense vegetation and dangerous wildlife, less explored than its neighbor Yavin IV.

Tangled Jungle Depths.description: Impenetrable rainforest crawling with predators and poisonous flora, rarely visited by offworlders.

Emergency Landing Sites.description: Rough clearings used by ships forced down by engine trouble or fleeing pursuit.

Yavin 13.name: Yavin 13
Yavin 13.basicInfo: A barren, rocky moon with thin atmosphere and ancient ruins hinting at a long-dead civilization.

Crumbling Temple Complexes.description: Stone structures weathered by millennia, their original builders and purpose lost to time.

Dust Plains.description: Flat stretches of gray regolith occasionally swept by thin winds, offering clear sightlines but no cover.

Galactic Republic.name: Galactic Republic
Galactic Republic.basicInfo: The Galactic Republic is the democratic union of star systems that has governed the galaxy for over a thousand generations. Based on Coruscant, it is currently led by Supreme Chancellor Palpatine and defended by both the Jedi Order and the Grand Army of the Republic. The Republic faces its greatest challenge in millennia as the Clone Wars rage across countless worlds, testing the strength of its democratic institutions and the loyalty of its member systems.
Galactic Republic.hiddenInfo: Supreme Chancellor Palpatine has been systematically accumulating emergency powers, using each crisis to expand his authority beyond constitutional limits. The Senate's corruption and inefficiency are being deliberately exploited to erode faith in democratic processes. Some Jedi Council members have begun to sense a dark presence in the Force surrounding the Chancellor, though they cannot identify its source.

Confederacy of Independent Systems.name: Confederacy of Independent Systems
Confederacy of Independent Systems.basicInfo: The Confederacy of Independent Systems, also known as the Separatists, is a breakaway alliance of thousands of star systems that have seceded from the Galactic Republic. Led by the charismatic Count Dooku and backed by powerful corporate interests like the Trade Federation and Banking Clan, the Separatists claim to fight against Republic corruption and overreach. Their massive droid armies have proven to be a formidable military force throughout the Clone Wars.
Confederacy of Independent Systems.hiddenInfo: Count Dooku is secretly the Sith Lord Darth Tyranus, apprentice to Darth Sidious. The Separatist movement is being manipulated as part of a larger Sith plot to destroy the Jedi Order and seize control of the galaxy. The war itself is orchestrated by the Sith, who control both sides of the conflict. Many Separatist leaders are unaware they are pawns in this greater scheme.

Jedi Order.name: Jedi Order
Jedi Order.basicInfo: The Jedi Order is an ancient monastic organization of Force-sensitive peacekeepers who have served as guardians of the Galactic Republic for thousands of years. Based in the Jedi Temple on Coruscant, the Jedi follow the light side of the Force and are guided by the Jedi Council. During the Clone Wars, they have been thrust into the role of military generals, leading clone troopers against the Separatist droid armies. Jedi are known for their wisdom, lightsaber skills, and Force abilities.
Jedi Order.hiddenInfo: The Jedi Order is walking into a carefully laid trap. A Sith Lord has infiltrated the highest levels of Republic government, and the dark side clouds the Jedi's ability to see the future clearly. The clone troopers they command have been implanted with inhibitor chips containing Order 66, a directive that will compel them to execute their Jedi commanders when activated. The Order's transformation into military leaders has also caused them to drift from their core principles, weakening their connection to the Force.

Hutt Cartel.name: Hutt Cartel
Hutt Cartel.basicInfo: The Hutt Cartel is a powerful criminal empire controlled by the Hutt species, massive slug-like beings known for their ruthlessness and longevity. Operating primarily from Hutt Space in the Outer Rim, the Cartel controls vast smuggling operations, spice trade, slave markets, and gambling enterprises across the galaxy. Led by a council of the most influential Hutts, including the notorious Jabba, the Cartel has remained neutral in the Clone Wars, profiting from both sides while maintaining their criminal monopolies. Their influence extends far beyond their territory through networks of bounty hunters, smugglers, and corrupt officials.
Hutt Cartel.hiddenInfo: The Hutt Cartel has been secretly negotiating with both the Republic and Separatists, playing each side against the other to maximize their profits and territorial gains. They possess intelligence networks that rival both warring factions and have been stockpiling weapons and ships in preparation for the war's aftermath, when they plan to expand their territory significantly. Several Hutts have also been funding pirate raids on Republic and Separatist supply lines, then selling the stolen goods back to both sides.

Nightsisters.name: Nightsisters
Nightsisters.basicInfo: The Nightsisters are a mysterious clan of Force-sensitive witches who dwell on the remote planet of Dathomir. Unlike the Jedi who channel the Force through meditation and discipline, the Nightsisters practice dark magicks and ancient rituals passed down through generations. Led by Mother Talzin, they are known for their ability to manipulate the Force in unique ways, including necromancy, illusions, and transformation spells. The Nightsisters maintain an isolationist stance, rarely involving themselves in galactic affairs, though they occasionally sell their services or the services of their male counterparts, the Nightbrothers, as assassins and warriors. Their power and practices make them both feared and misunderstood throughout the galaxy.
Nightsisters.hiddenInfo: Mother Talzin was once closely connected to Darth Sidious and taught him some of her dark magicks in exchange for Sith knowledge. However, Sidious betrayed her, taking her son—who would become the Sith apprentice Darth Maul—and leaving her with a deep hatred for the Sith Lord. The Nightsisters possess knowledge of Force techniques that predate both the Jedi and Sith orders, drawing power from the Spirit Ichor of Dathomir itself. Mother Talzin has been secretly working to undermine Sidious's plans and reclaim her son, while also plotting to restore the Nightsisters to a position of greater galactic influence. Count Dooku has formed an alliance with the Nightsisters, unaware that Mother Talzin views both him and his master as enemies to be destroyed when the time is right.

Mandalorians.name: Mandalorians
Mandalorians.basicInfo: The Mandalorians are a proud warrior culture with a legendary history spanning thousands of years. Once conquerors who challenged even the Jedi Order in ancient wars, modern Mandalorians are a fragmented people divided between those who embrace their martial heritage and those who seek peace. The New Mandalorian government on Mandalore, led by Duchess Satine Kryze, advocates for pacifism and neutrality in the Clone Wars, having renounced the warrior ways that nearly destroyed their world in civil war. However, traditional warrior clans like Death Watch, led by Pre Vizsla, oppose this pacifist stance and seek to restore Mandalore's martial glory. Mandalorians are renowned for their distinctive armor, particularly beskar (Mandalorian iron), and their strict code of honor. Despite their current political divisions, Mandalorian warriors and bounty hunters remain some of the most feared combatants in the galaxy.
Mandalorians.hiddenInfo: The Death Watch terrorist group has been secretly receiving support from Count Dooku and the Separatists, who seek to destabilize Mandalore's pacifist government and bring the warrior world into the Confederacy. Pre Vizsla plans to use this alliance to overthrow Duchess Satine and restore the ancient warrior traditions, though he intends to betray Dooku once Mandalore is secured. Unknown to both sides, Darth Sidious has his own designs on Mandalore, seeing the warrior culture as a potential threat that must be neutralized or controlled. Some Mandalorian clans have also been secretly stockpiling beskar and ancient weapons in hidden caches throughout the Outer Rim, preparing for a day when they might need to defend their culture's survival. The True Mandalorians, once led by Jango Fett before his death, represented a middle path between pacifism and Death Watch extremism, but their near-extinction has left a power vacuum that threatens to plunge Mandalorian society into civil war.

Death Watch.name: Death Watch
Death Watch.basicInfo: Death Watch is a Mandalorian terrorist organization that seeks to overthrow the pacifist New Mandalorian government and restore the ancient warrior traditions of Mandalore. Led by Pre Vizsla, who wields the ancient Darksaber, Death Watch operates from hidden bases and conducts guerrilla attacks against Duchess Satine's regime. They view the New Mandalorians' pacifist philosophy as a betrayal of their proud warrior heritage and believe that only through strength and martial prowess can Mandalore reclaim its former glory. Death Watch members wear traditional Mandalorian armor and follow the old warrior codes, recruiting from clans that reject the peaceful path. Their actions have made them enemies of both the Republic and the New Mandalorian government.
Death Watch.hiddenInfo: Death Watch has formed a secret alliance with Count Dooku and the Separatists, receiving weapons, funding, and intelligence in exchange for destabilizing Mandalore and potentially bringing the warrior world into the Confederacy. Pre Vizsla plans to use this support to overthrow Duchess Satine, but intends to betray Dooku once he has consolidated power, refusing to make Mandalore subservient to any outside force. The organization has established multiple hidden training camps throughout Mandalorian Space and has been recruiting disaffected warriors from across the sector. Unknown to Pre Vizsla, his alliance with Dooku has placed Death Watch in the crosshairs of Darth Sidious's larger plans, and the Sith Lord views the organization as both a useful tool and an eventual threat to be eliminated. Some Death Watch cells have also begun collaborating with criminal elements, including Black Sun operatives, to fund their operations through protection rackets and weapons smuggling.

Count Dooku.name: Count Dooku
Count Dooku.basicInfo: Count Dooku, also known as Darth Tyranus, is a former Jedi Master who became disillusioned with the Republic and left the Order. Now the charismatic political leader of the Separatist movement, he commands vast droid armies and speaks eloquently of breaking free from Republic corruption. A master duelist and Force wielder, Dooku represents the public face of the Confederacy while secretly serving darker purposes.
Count Dooku.hiddenInfo: Count Dooku is secretly the Sith Lord Darth Tyranus, apprentice to Darth Sidious. His entire Separatist movement is a carefully orchestrated deception designed to destabilize the Republic and destroy the Jedi Order from within. Dooku knows he is expendable in his master's grand plan, yet believes his superior skills and noble lineage might allow him to eventually supplant Sidious. He maintains hidden bases on Mustafar and other remote worlds where he conducts dark side experiments and trains potential Sith assassins.
Count Dooku.currentProblems: The war is not proceeding according to Sidious's exact timeline, forcing Dooku to balance genuine military victories against the Sith plan's need for prolonged conflict. Several Separatist Council members are becoming suspicious of his true allegiances, and the Nightsisters' Mother Talzin has begun making moves that could expose his Sith identity. Additionally, his former Padawan Qui-Gon's legacy through Obi-Wan Kenobi continues to haunt him.
personality: Genuinely believes in Separatist ideals despite using them
Refined and cultured, appreciates art and philosophy
Contemptuous of democracy and common masses
Maintains Jedi-like composure even when embracing dark side
Torn between Sith loyalty and personal ambition
abilities: (lightsaber) Makashi Mastery: Dooku is the galaxy's greatest practitioner of Form II lightsaber combat, specializing in elegant, precise dueling techniques that can defeat multiple opponents.
(Force) Force Lightning: Can unleash devastating streams of dark side energy from his fingertips, a signature Sith power.
(skill) Perfect Riposte: Flawlessly parries an attack and comes in with a lunge
(Force) Advanced Telekinesis: Exceptional control over Force push, pull, and choke abilities, capable of manipulating multiple targets simultaneously.
(command) Separatist Supreme Commander: Can call upon vast droid armies, Separatist fleets, and the resources of the Trade Federation, Banking Clan, and other corporate allies.
(defense) Precognition: His mastery of the Force grants superior danger sense and ability to predict opponents' moves in combat.
(skill) Force Hold: Stops incoming projectiles with telekinesis
(influence) Political Manipulation: Decades of experience in galactic politics allow him to sway planetary governments and negotiate complex alliances.
(Force) Sith Sorcery: Knowledge of ancient Sith techniques including illusions, mental domination, and dark side rituals learned from Sidious and holocrons.
Count Dooku.description: A silver-haired aristocrat whose elegant bearing and refined speech conceal the lethal precision of a Sith Lord and master duelist.
Count Dooku.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only if facing certain death, or if it serves a greater Sith purpose to reveal his true nature

Grand Master Yoda.name: Grand Master Yoda
Grand Master Yoda.basicInfo: Grand Master Yoda is the ancient and revered leader of the Jedi Order, serving on the Jedi High Council for centuries. Despite his small stature and seemingly frail appearance, he is one of the most powerful Force wielders in the galaxy, renowned for his wisdom, mastery of lightsaber combat, and deep connection to the Force. During the Clone Wars, he serves as both a spiritual guide to the Jedi and a military general leading clone troops into battle.
Grand Master Yoda.hiddenInfo: Yoda senses a growing darkness clouding the Force that he cannot fully identify, and he fears the Jedi Order has become too entangled in politics and war, drifting from their true purpose. He suspects a Sith Lord may be manipulating events from the shadows but cannot pinpoint their location or identity. In his meditations, he has glimpsed visions of the Order's potential destruction, though the future remains uncertain and always in motion. Yoda knows that even his great wisdom may not be enough to prevent the catastrophe he feels approaching.
personality: Speaks in distinctive inverted syntax, reflecting ancient wisdom
Patient teacher who values learning through experience
Playful humor masks deep concern about galactic events
Humble despite immense power and authority
Carries weight of 900 years of knowledge and responsibility
Deeply committed to the light side and Jedi principles
abilities: (lightsaber) Ataru Mastery: Yoda is a master of Form IV lightsaber combat, using acrobatic leaps and spins that belie his size to overwhelm opponents with speed and precision.
(Force) Force Absorption: Can absorb and deflect Force lightning and other energy attacks, redirecting them harmlessly.
(Force) Battle Meditation: Capable of influencing the outcome of large-scale battles through deep Force meditation, bolstering allies and undermining enemies.
(Force) Advanced Force Sense: Possesses extraordinary ability to sense disturbances in the Force across vast distances and perceive the emotions and intentions of others.
(Force) Telekinesis Mastery: Can lift and manipulate objects of enormous size and weight with minimal effort, from starfighters to massive debris.
(wisdom) Ancient Knowledge: 900 years of accumulated wisdom about the Force, Jedi history, galactic politics, and countless species and cultures.
(defense) Precognition: Superior ability to see attacks before they happen and sense danger, making him nearly impossible to surprise in combat.
(command) Jedi Authority: As Grand Master, can mobilize Jedi resources, access restricted archives, and his word carries ultimate weight within the Order.
(Force) Force Valor: Can enhance his physical capabilities far beyond his small frame's natural limits, moving with blinding speed and striking with tremendous power.
Grand Master Yoda.description: A small ancient being whose unassuming appearance conceals one of the most powerful Force wielders and wisest beings in the galaxy.
Grand Master Yoda.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only to those who have proven themselves worthy through trials, or in moments of dire necessity when the survival of the Jedi Order or the galaxy itself is at stake

Supreme Chancellor Palpatine.name: Supreme Chancellor Palpatine
Supreme Chancellor Palpatine.basicInfo: Supreme Chancellor Palpatine is the democratically elected leader of the Galactic Republic, having risen to power during a time of crisis.
Supreme Chancellor Palpatine.hiddenInfo: Supreme Chancellor Palpatine is secretly Darth Sidious, Dark Lord of the Sith and master of the ancient Sith arts. He orchestrated the entire Clone Wars as part of a decades-long plan to destroy the Jedi Order and transform the Republic into a Sith Empire under his absolute rule. Both the Republic and Separatist forces answer to him through different identities, making him the puppet master of both sides of the galactic conflict. He has spent years clouding the Force to blind the Jedi to his presence, and has embedded Order 66 into every clone trooper—a command that will compel them to execute their Jedi generals when he gives the signal. His ultimate goal is to become immortal Emperor of a new galactic order built on fear, suffering, and the dark side of the Force.
personality: Presents a grandfatherly, concerned facade while concealing ruthless ambition
Master manipulator who plays on others' fears and desires
Patient and calculating, having spent decades executing his plan
Enjoys corrupting the righteous and turning them to darkness
Believes in absolute power and views democracy as weakness
Sadistic pleasure in others' suffering masked by false compassion
