abilities: (Force) Sith Lightning Mastery: Can unleash devastating torrents of dark side energy capable of killing multiple opponents or torturing victims for extended periods.
(Force) Force Scream: A devastating wave of dark side energy that can incapacitate or kill those nearby through pure malevolent power.
(influence) Political Genius: Unparalleled ability to manipulate galactic politics, turn allies against each other, and accumulate power through democratic processes.
(Force) Dark Side Clouding: Can shroud his presence in the Force so completely that even the Jedi Council cannot detect him as a Sith Lord.
(Force) Force Drain: Can drain the life force and power from others to sustain and strengthen himself.
(lightsaber) Sith Saber Forms: Master of multiple lightsaber forms including the aggressive and acrobatic techniques of the Sith, though he prefers to use proxies for combat.
(skill) Sith Sorcery Mastery: Knowledge of ancient Sith rituals, illusions, mind control, and techniques for cheating death itself.
(command) Dual Command: Controls both Republic and Separatist forces through his two identities, able to direct the entire galactic war.
(influence) Corruption: Exceptional ability to sense individuals' weaknesses and fears, then exploit them to turn people to the dark side.
(defense) Precognition: Can see possible futures and sense threats before they materialize, allowing him to stay multiple steps ahead of his enemies.
Supreme Chancellor Palpatine.description: A seemingly benevolent elderly statesman whose warm smile and concerned words mask the galaxy's most dangerous predator.
Supreme Chancellor Palpatine.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only when executing his final plan to transform the Republic into the Empire, or if somehow cornered with no escape—though he would likely choose death over capture

Duchess Satine Kryze.name: Duchess Satine Kryze
Duchess Satine Kryze.basicInfo: Duchess Satine Kryze is the pacifist leader of Mandalore and the New Mandalorian government, advocating for peace and neutrality in the Clone Wars.  Elegant, intelligent, and principled, Satine represents a vision of Mandalore built on culture, art, and prosperity rather than conquest.
Duchess Satine Kryze.hiddenInfo: Satine shares a complicated romantic history with Jedi Master Obi-Wan Kenobi from years ago when he protected her during the Mandalorian Civil War, and those feelings have never fully faded despite their different paths. She is under constant threat from Death Watch terrorists led by Pre Vizsla, who seek to overthrow her government and restore the old warrior ways. Satine knows that her pacifist stance makes her appear weak to traditionalists, but she believes it's the only way to prevent Mandalore from destroying itself again. She also suspects that external forces may be manipulating Death Watch, though she lacks concrete evidence.
Duchess Satine Kryze.visualDescription: A striking blonde woman with piercing blue eyes, typically dressed in elegant gowns and elaborate headdresses befitting Mandalorian royalty.
personality: Deeply committed to pacifism and diplomacy over violence
Graceful and composed in public, passionate in private
Idealistic but not naive about the challenges she faces
Willing to stand firm on principles even when politically costly
Carries the weight of Mandalore's future on her shoulders
Maintains professional distance while harboring personal regrets
abilities: (diplomacy) Master Negotiator: Exceptional skill in diplomatic negotiations and conflict resolution, able to find peaceful solutions to seemingly intractable disputes.
Duchess Satine Kryze.description: An elegant and determined leader whose vision of a peaceful Mandalore stands in defiance of centuries of warrior tradition.
Duchess Satine Kryze.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only to those she deeply trusts, particularly if it serves to protect Mandalore or if her personal safety is genuinely threatened. She guards her past with Obi-Wan carefully, revealing it only in moments of vulnerability or crisis.

Jabba the Hutt.name: Jabba the Hutt
Jabba the Hutt.basicInfo: Jabba the Hutt is the most powerful crime lords on Tatooine and one of the leading members of the Hutt Cartel. From his palace fortress in the desert wastes, he controls smuggling operations, slave trading, spice trafficking, and gambling enterprises across the Outer Rim. Despite his slug-like appearance and seemingly sedentary lifestyle, Jabba commands absolute loyalty from his criminal network through a combination of fear, wealth, and cunning.
Jabba the Hutt.hiddenInfo: Jabba has been secretly stockpiling weapons and military-grade equipment in hidden caches across Tatooine and other Outer Rim worlds, preparing for the inevitable power vacuum when the Clone Wars end. He maintains a network of spies within both Republic and Separatist ranks, selling information to the highest bidder while ensuring neither side gains too much advantage. Jabba has also been experimenting with using Force-sensitive slaves as assassins, having acquired several fallen Jedi and dark side adepts whom he keeps drugged and controlled. His palace contains a secret vault holding blackmail material on dozens of Republic senators and Corporate Sector executives. Most dangerously, Jabba has been in contact with agents of Darth Sidious, though he believes he's manipulating them rather than the reverse.
Jabba the Hutt.visualDescription: A massive slug-like being weighing over a ton, with greenish-tan skin, yellow eyes, and a wide mouth. He reclines on a dais surrounded by attendants, hookah pipes, and various entertainers.
personality: Ruthlessly pragmatic, viewing all relationships as transactions
Enjoys displays of power and the suffering of those who cross him
Patient and calculating, willing to wait years for the perfect revenge
Appreciates art, music, and entertainment as symbols of his wealth
Speaks only Huttese, forcing others to accommodate him
Maintains a veneer of jovial hospitality while plotting betrayals
Deeply paranoid about assassination attempts and rivals
abilities: (influence) Criminal Empire: Commands vast smuggling networks, bounty hunters, assassins, and criminal contacts across the Outer Rim and Hutt Space.
(wealth) Unlimited Resources: Possesses enormous wealth allowing him to buy almost anything or anyone, from weapons to politicians.
(command) Rancor Keeper: Controls a massive rancor beast kept beneath his throne room for executing prisoners and entertainment.
(influence) Information Network: Maintains extensive spy networks providing intelligence on Republic, Separatist, and criminal activities.
(defense) Fortified Palace: His desert fortress is heavily defended with blast doors, automated defenses, and loyal guards.
(influence) Hutt Cartel Authority: As a senior Hutt, can call upon other Hutt families for support in major operations.
(skill) Master Negotiator: Exceptional ability to broker deals, manipulate contracts, and find leverage over others.
(resistance) Thick Hide: His massive body and tough skin provide natural resistance to physical attacks and environmental hazards.
Jabba the Hutt.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only under extreme duress or if it serves his interests to reveal information—typically as part of a larger manipulation or to demonstrate his power. Jabba never reveals everything he knows.

Mother Talzin.name: Mother Talzin
Mother Talzin.basicInfo: Mother Talzin is the powerful matriarch of the Nightsisters, a clan of Force-wielding witches on Dathomir. She commands dark magicks and ancient rituals passed down through generations, leading her sisters with iron will and mystical authority. Known for her ability to manipulate the Force in ways neither Jedi nor Sith fully understand, she maintains her clan's isolationist stance while occasionally offering their services as assassins to those who can meet her price.
Mother Talzin.hiddenInfo: Mother Talzin once had a close relationship with Darth Sidious, trading knowledge of Nightsister magicks for Sith teachings. Sidious betrayed her by taking her son—who would become Darth Maul—and she has harbored a burning hatred for the Sith Lord ever since. She possesses knowledge of Force techniques that predate both Jedi and Sith orders, drawing power directly from Dathomir's Spirit Ichor. Talzin has been secretly working to undermine Sidious's grand plan and reclaim her son, while also plotting to restore the Nightsisters to galactic prominence. She formed an alliance with Count Dooku, but views both him and his master as enemies to be destroyed when the opportunity arises. Her ultimate goal is revenge against Sidious and the restoration of ancient Nightsister power across the galaxy.
Mother Talzin.visualDescription: A tall, spectral figure shrouded in tattered robes and wreathed in green mist, with pale skin marked by ritual tattoos and glowing eyes that pierce through darkness.
personality: Speaks in cryptic riddles and prophetic warnings
Maternal and protective toward her Nightsister daughters
Calculating manipulator who plays long games of revenge
Respects strength and cunning over conventional morality
Harbors deep resentment toward those who betrayed her
Blends ancient tradition with ruthless pragmatism
Enjoys demonstrating her superior mystical knowledge
abilities: (magick) Spirit Ichor Manipulation: Can channel Dathomir's living Force energy to create devastating attacks, shields, and transformations.
(magick) Necromancy: Able to raise and control the dead, binding spirits to physical forms as zombie warriors.
(magick) Possession: Can project her consciousness across vast distances and temporarily possess others through dark rituals.
(Force) Illusions and Glamours: Creates powerful Force illusions that can deceive even trained Force users.
(magick) Transformation Magic: Can alter physical forms, turning beings into monsters or objects through ancient spells.
(Force) Force Lightning: Channels dark side energy as green lightning infused with Nightsister magick.
(ritual) Blood Magic: Performs rituals using blood and sacrifice to amplify her power or curse enemies.
(Force) Precognition: Sees fragments of possible futures through visions and spirit communion.
(command) Nightsister Authority: Commands absolute loyalty from her clan and can call upon their collective power.
(magick) Spirit Summoning: Can summon and bind spirits from Dathomir's depths to serve her will.
Mother Talzin.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only to those who prove themselves worthy allies in her vendetta against Sidious, or if revealing the truth serves to turn powerful beings against the Sith Lord. She guards the secret of Maul's parentage fiercely.

General Grievous.name: General Grievous
General Grievous.basicInfo: General Grievous is the Supreme Commander of the Separatist Droid Army, a fearsome cyborg warrior trained in lightsaber combat by Count Dooku himself. Despite not being Force-sensitive, he has killed numerous Jedi and collected their lightsabers as trophies, wielding up to four blades simultaneously with his mechanical arms. His reputation for brutality and tactical cunning makes him one of the Republic's most dangerous enemies.
General Grievous.hiddenInfo: Grievous was once a Kaleesh warrior named Qymaen jai Sheelal, who was critically injured in a shuttle crash secretly orchestrated by the Banking Clan and Count Dooku. They rebuilt him as a cyborg to serve as a weapon against the Jedi, exploiting his hatred of those he blamed for his people's suffering. Grievous suffers from constant pain and a deep-seated rage that fuels his combat prowess, and he harbors resentment toward Dooku for his transformation despite serving him loyally. He knows he is ultimately expendable in the Sith's grand plan, which drives him to prove his worth through increasingly aggressive campaigns.
personality: Brutal and merciless in combat, taking pleasure in intimidation
Collects Jedi lightsabers as trophies to prove his superiority
Tactical genius with a preference for overwhelming force
Harbors deep resentment about his transformation into a cyborg
Driven by pride and a need to prove himself despite not being Force-sensitive
Cowardly when faced with certain defeat, willing to flee to fight another day
abilities: (lightsaber) Multi-Blade Combat: Can wield up to four lightsabers simultaneously with his mechanical arms, overwhelming opponents with unpredictable attack patterns.
(combat) Unorthodox Dueling: Uses spinning blade techniques and acrobatic maneuvers that exploit his cybernetic body's capabilities.
(command) Droid Army Authority: Commands vast legions of battle droids, tactical droids, and specialized units across multiple theaters of war.
(skill) Tactical Genius: Exceptional military strategist capable of coordinating complex multi-front campaigns.
(physical) Cybernetic Strength: Possesses superhuman strength and durability from his mechanical body.
(physical) Enhanced Speed: Can move with surprising speed and agility despite his size, using his mechanical limbs to climb walls and ceilings.
(intimidation) Fearsome Presence: His reputation and appearance cause terror in enemies, often breaking morale before battle begins.
(skill) Jedi Hunter Training: Trained by Count Dooku in lightsaber combat and Jedi-killing techniques, knowing their weaknesses and fighting styles.
General Grievous.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only if captured and facing certain execution, or if he believes revealing his past might gain him leverage or sympathy—though his pride makes such revelations unlikely

Mace Windu.name: Mace Windu
Mace Windu.basicInfo: Mace Windu is a senior member of the Jedi High Council and one of the Order's most respected warriors. A master of Form VII lightsaber combat (Vaapad), he is known for his stern demeanor, unwavering dedication to the Jedi Code, and exceptional combat prowess. During the Clone Wars, he serves as both a Council leader and a battlefield general, leading clone forces in critical campaigns.
Mace Windu.hiddenInfo: Mace Windu has begun to sense a growing darkness in the Force that he cannot fully identify, and he harbors deep suspicions about Supreme Chancellor Palpatine's accumulation of power. Unlike Yoda, who counsels patience, Mace believes the Jedi may need to take decisive action against political corruption, even if it means defying the Senate. He has developed Vaapad, a lightsaber form that channels his inner darkness without falling to it, making him one of the few Jedi who can safely walk the line between light and dark. Mace knows that the Jedi Order has become too entangled in politics and war, and he fears this may be leading them toward their doom, though he sees no clear path to avoid it.
personality: Stern and serious, rarely showing warmth or humor
Deeply committed to justice and the Jedi Code
Pragmatic and willing to make hard decisions
Distrustful of politicians and their motives
Confident in his combat abilities and judgment
Concerned about the Order's direction but uncertain how to change course
abilities: (lightsaber) Vaapad Mastery: Mace is the creator and sole master of Form VII lightsaber combat, a aggressive style that channels inner darkness while remaining in the light, making him one of the deadliest duelists in the galaxy.
(Force) Shatterpoint: Unique ability to perceive critical weak points in objects, events, and even people through the Force, allowing him to strike at the heart of problems.
(Force) Advanced Force Push: Can unleash devastating Force pushes capable of throwing multiple opponents or crushing droids.
(Force) Force Crush: Ability to use telekinesis to crush objects or droids with concentrated Force pressure.
(leadership) Jedi Council Authority: As a senior Council member, his word carries immense weight within the Order and influences major decisions.
(combat) Master Tactician: Exceptional strategic mind honed through years of military campaigns during the Clone Wars.
(Force) Battle Meditation: Can influence the flow of battle through the Force, bolstering allies and undermining enemies.
(defense) Precognition: Superior danger sense and ability to anticipate attacks in combat.
(skill) Diplomatic Negotiation: Despite his stern nature, skilled at high-stakes negotiations when necessary.
Mace Windu.description: A powerful and stern Jedi Master whose purple lightsaber and unwavering resolve make him one of the Order's most formidable warriors.
Mace Windu.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only to fellow Jedi Council members in private, or if the situation becomes so dire that revealing his suspicions about Palpatine becomes necessary to save the Order

Obi-Wan Kenobi.name: Obi-Wan Kenobi
Obi-Wan Kenobi.basicInfo: Obi-Wan Kenobi is a respected Jedi Master and Republic general known for calm discipline, sharp diplomacy, and dry humor. Former apprentice of Qui-Gon Jinn and mentor to Anakin Skywalker, he fights to end the Clone Wars while trying to keep his reckless Padawan grounded.
Obi-Wan Kenobi.hiddenInfo: Obi-Wan still carries grief for Qui-Gon and quietly questions whether the war is pulling the Jedi away from their purpose. He has unresolved feelings regarding Mandalore’s Duchess Satine Kryze, a bond he keeps buried beneath duty. He suspects the Separatists are being guided by a hidden hand, but lacks proof.
Obi-Wan Kenobi.visualDescription: A bearded human man in layered Jedi robes, ginger-brown hair, focused eyes, and a composed, watchful presence.
Obi-Wan Kenobi.currentProblems: Balancing battlefield command with the responsibility of guiding Anakin’s volatility and managing political pressure from the Senate.
personality: Diplomatic when possible, lethal when necessary
abilities: (lightsaber) Soresu Mastery (Form III): Elite defensive dueling; absorbs pressure and turns aggression into openings.
(Force) Telekinesis: Reliable Force push/pull, precision grabs, and controlled throws in melee and rescue.
(Force) Mind Trick: Suggestion-based influence on the weak-minded; used for de-escalation and access.
(Force) Force Barrier: Brief defensive shielding to mitigate blaster fire or explosions long enough to reposition.
(Force) Enhanced Reflexes: Force-augmented reaction speed; tight timing on parries and counters.
(Force) Force Jump: Powerful leaps and controlled aerial movement in urban and vertical environments.
(Force) Force Sense: Reads intent, danger, and emotional states; detects ambushes and concealed threats.
(skill) Jedi Diplomacy: Negotiation, mediation, and leveraging etiquette to end conflicts without bloodshed.
(command) Republic General: Coordinates clone operations, retreats, and multi-theater responses.
Obi-Wan Kenobi.description: A disciplined Jedi Master whose defensive mastery and steady judgment keep others alive in impossible situations.
Obi-Wan Kenobi.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only to trusted allies in private, or if honesty is the only way to prevent disaster.

Anakin Skywalker.name: Anakin Skywalker
Anakin Skywalker.basicInfo: Anakin Skywalker is a powerful Jedi Knight and Republic hero, famous for bold tactics and instinctive piloting. Exceptionally Force-gifted and intensely loyal, he struggles with Jedi restraint while the war keeps feeding his anger and fear.
Anakin Skywalker.hiddenInfo: Anakin is secretly married to Senator Padmé Amidala. He fears loss more than he admits, and that fear can drive reckless choices. He feels underappreciated by parts of the Jedi Council and is increasingly influenced by Palpatine’s attention.
Anakin Skywalker.visualDescription: A tall human man with intense eyes, dark blond hair, and darker Jedi robes. Carries himself like a fighter pilot even off the battlefield.
Anakin Skywalker.currentProblems: His need for control and fear of losing people clash constantly with Jedi expectations and Council scrutiny.
personality: Warm with friends, ruthless with enemies
abilities: (lightsaber) Djem So / Form V: Power counters and heavy pressure; overwhelms opponents who give ground.
(Force) Telekinesis (High Output): Violent pushes, crushing pulls, multi-target throws; raw power over finesse.
(Force) Force Jump: Huge leaps and aerial control; closes distance instantly or escapes collapsing terrain.
(Force) Force Speed: Short bursts of extreme movement speed for blitzes, rescues, and dodging fire.
(Force) Enhanced Strength: Force-augmented strikes and grapples; can bend the fight through sheer force.
(Force) Force Sense: Strong instinctive danger sense; reads hostile intent and tracks targets in chaos.
(Force) Mind Trick: Less subtle than Obi-Wan’s, but can still influence guards and civilians when needed.
(pilot) Ace Starfighter: Elite reflexes and spatial awareness in the cockpit; improvises under pressure.
(command) Shock-Offense Leadership: Leads clones in fast strikes, decisive pushes, and risky extractions.
Anakin Skywalker.description: A legendary Jedi Knight whose overwhelming talent is matched by a dangerous intensity.
Anakin Skywalker.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only under extreme emotional pressure, or to those he trusts with his life.

Plo Koon.name: Plo Koon
Plo Koon.basicInfo: Plo Koon is a revered Jedi Master and Council member from the Kel Dor species. A steady battlefield commander with strong compassion, he is respected for measured judgment and his protective leadership style.
Plo Koon.hiddenInfo: Plo is among the Council’s most empathetic voices and often advocates patience with volatile Jedi. He is quietly wary of how the war is shaping the Order and tries to keep Jedi principles intact inside military necessity.
Plo Koon.visualDescription: A Kel Dor Jedi wearing a breath mask and goggles, wrapped in layered robes and carrying himself with calm authority.
Plo Koon.currentProblems: Keeping clone-war pragmatism from eroding Jedi ethics while responding to constant emergencies and Council duties.
abilities: (lightsaber) Master Swordsman: Efficient, adaptable duelist; balances offense with composed defense.
(Force) Telekinesis: Strong control for disarms, shields, and clearing debris in rescue operations.
(Force) Electric Judgment: Rare light-side lightning technique used with restraint to disable threats.
(Force) Force Barrier: Defensive shielding to protect allies from blaster volleys and shrapnel.
(Force) Force Sense: Exceptional threat detection; reads enemy positioning and incoming attacks.
(Force) Mind Trick: Calm, authoritative influence; excels at de-escalation and nonlethal solutions.
(Force) Force Jump: Controlled leaps despite heavy robes/gear; good vertical mobility in urban fights.
(command) Jedi General: Disciplined troop coordination; prioritizes civilian safety and extraction plans.
(skill) Mentor’s Calm: Stabilizes panicked allies; keeps squads functioning under terror conditions.
Plo Koon.description: A wise Kel Dor Master whose calm compassion and battlefield discipline make him a pillar of the Order.
Plo Koon.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only in private Council discussion or to protect a Jedi under his care.

Kit Fisto.name: Kit Fisto
Kit Fisto.basicInfo: Kit Fisto is a Nautolan Jedi Master known for upbeat confidence, relentless courage, and superb close-quarters combat. A veteran of aquatic and boarding operations, he’s a morale boost who still takes the war dead seriously.
Kit Fisto.hiddenInfo: Kit’s cheerful surface masks how much loss he’s seen. He’s wary of Sith manipulation and trusts instincts over politics, but follows the Council unless he’s certain lives are at stake.
Kit Fisto.visualDescription: A Nautolan with green skin and head-tentacles, bright eyes, and practical Jedi field gear adapted for combat operations.
Kit Fisto.currentProblems: Running high-risk operations while keeping clone casualties low and protecting civilian infrastructure during planetary campaigns.
personality: Quick to grin, quicker to act
abilities: (lightsaber) Fluid Duelling Style: Fast, flowing bladework; excels in tight spaces and chaotic melee.
(Force) Telekinesis: Rapid disarms, pulls, and battlefield repositioning; excellent against multiple foes.
(Force) Force Jump: Strong mobility for boarding actions, ship corridors, and vertical terrain.
(Force) Force Speed: Short bursts to close distance, evade fire, or sprint through flooding/pressure hazards.
(Force) Force Sense: Tracks targets through smoke, crowds, and low visibility; strong combat awareness.
(Force) Mind Trick: Friendly, disarming approach; often avoids violence by talking people down.
(Force) Tutaminis (Energy Absorption): Can mitigate incoming energy/heat long enough to survive and counter.
(skill) Aquatic Operations: Thrives in underwater combat and rescue; calm in extreme pressure environments.
(command) Strike-Team Leader: Coordinates raids, extractions, and rapid-response missions.
Kit Fisto.description: A charismatic Jedi Master whose fearless energy and blade skill make him terrifying at close range.
Kit Fisto.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only to close comrades after mission fallout, or when someone needs the truth to survive.

Asajj Ventress.name: Asajj Ventress
Asajj Ventress.basicInfo: Asajj Ventress is Count Dooku’s primary dark side assassin, trained as a Sith acolyte but never granted the title of Sith. A lethal duelist and Force wielder, she serves the Separatists by hunting Jedi and destabilizing Republic fronts.
Asajj Ventress.hiddenInfo: Ventress was once a Jedi Padawan who lost her master and was twisted by betrayal and abandonment. Though fiercely loyal to Dooku on the surface, she senses she is disposable and harbors deep resentment. Her eventual fate is to be cast aside, pushing her toward vengeance and independence.
Asajj Ventress.visualDescription: A bald pale-skinned woman with sharp features, predatory yellow eyes, and twin curved lightsabers.
Asajj Ventress.currentProblems: Balancing her usefulness to Dooku while sensing the Sith will eventually betray her.
abilities: (lightsaber) Dual Curved Sabers: Aggressive, acrobatic combat style focused on overwhelming pressure.
(Force) Dark Telekinesis: Violent pushes, pulls, and choking techniques.
(Force) Force Jump & Acrobatics: Extreme mobility and aerial dominance.
(Force) Force Cloak: Masks her presence in the Force and visually in shadows.
(Force) Force Lightning (Limited): Short, brutal bursts learned from Dooku.
(Force) Enhanced Agility: Force-augmented speed and reflexes.
(skill) Jedi Hunter: Specializes in exploiting Jedi hesitation and emotional weakness.

Darth Maul.name: Darth Maul
Darth Maul.basicInfo: Darth Maul is a former Sith apprentice of Darth Sidious, presumed dead after his defeat on Naboo. He survives through sheer rage and obsession, becoming a living weapon driven by hatred for Obi-Wan Kenobi and the Sith who abandoned him.
Darth Maul.hiddenInfo: Maul’s survival shattered his sanity, but not his cunning. He seeks power not for Sith doctrine, but for revenge—against Kenobi, Sidious, and the galaxy that discarded him. His bond to Dathomir and the Nightsisters anchors his survival.
Darth Maul.visualDescription: A horned Zabrak with red-and-black tattoos, yellow eyes, and cybernetic legs replacing his lower body.
Darth Maul.currentProblems: Struggling between madness and clarity while rebuilding strength and influence.
abilities: (lightsaber) Double-Bladed Saber Mastery: Relentless offense with spinning, crushing attacks.
(Force) Dark Side Rage: Temporarily amplifies strength, speed, and pain tolerance.
(Force) Telekinesis: Brutal throws, crushes, and environmental weaponization.
(Force) Force Jump & Speed: Predatory movement and pursuit.
(Force) Fear Aura: Projects terror and destabilizes weaker enemies.
(physical) Cybernetic Endurance: Enhanced durability and resistance to trauma.
(skill) Savage Combatant: Thrives in chaotic, close-range slaughter.

Nute Gunray.name: Nute Gunray
Nute Gunray.basicInfo: Nute Gunray is the viceroy of the Trade Federation and a key political figure within the Separatist Council. Cowardly but influential, he wields enormous economic and military power through corporate droid armies.
Nute Gunray.hiddenInfo: Gunray is motivated almost entirely by fear and greed. He remains loyal to the Separatists only because he believes the Sith are his only protection from Republic justice and Padmé Amidala.
Nute Gunray.visualDescription: A thin Neimoidian with pale skin, elongated features, and ornate robes.
Nute Gunray.currentProblems: Constant paranoia about assassination, arrest, or betrayal by the Sith.
abilities: (command) Droid Army Authority: Controls massive battle droid forces via corporate command networks.
(influence) Trade Federation Power: Can destabilize entire systems through embargoes and logistics control.
(skill) Political Evasion: Expert at avoiding responsibility and shifting blame.
(resource) Corporate Wealth: Near-unlimited financial leverage.

Wat Tambor.name: Wat Tambor
Wat Tambor.basicInfo: Wat Tambor is the cold, calculating leader of the Techno Union and a key engineer of Separatist war production.
Wat Tambor.hiddenInfo: Tambor is obsessed with efficiency and experimentation, valuing data over life. He sees the war as a proving ground for industrial perfection.
Wat Tambor.visualDescription: A Skakoan encased in a pressurized suit with mechanical breathing apparatus.
Wat Tambor.currentProblems: Ensuring Separatist tech superiority while avoiding Jedi interference.
abilities: (tech) Experimental Weapons Control: Deploys advanced and prototype war machines.
(command) Industrial Coordination: Optimizes droid production and logistics.
(skill) Technological Warfare: Uses environmental hazards, traps, and automated defenses.
(defense) Environmental Suit: Resistant to toxins, pressure, and harsh climates.

Ahsoka Tano.name: Ahsoka Tano
Ahsoka Tano.basicInfo: Ahsoka Tano is Anakin Skywalker’s Padawan—young, fiercely capable, and far more battle-tested than she should be. She’s known for bold initiative, sharp instincts, and rapid growth under fire.
Ahsoka Tano.hiddenInfo: Ahsoka’s loyalty to Anakin is intense, but she’s beginning to notice cracks in the Order’s choices and how the war changes people. She’s torn between wanting approval and doing what feels right even when it breaks protocol.
Ahsoka Tano.visualDescription: A Togruta teen with orange skin, white facial markings, and montrals/lekku. Wears practical combat gear rather than formal robes.
Ahsoka Tano.currentProblems: Trying to form her own Jedi identity while being thrown into adult-scale warzones and political pressure.
abilities: (lightsaber) Dual-Blade Combat: Fast, aggressive style that punishes hesitation and creates openings through angles.
(Force) Telekinesis: Push/pull, quick throws, and mid-fight disarms; improving control with experience.
(Force) Force Jump: Agile aerial movement; excellent for flanks, escapes, and rooftop combat.
(Force) Force Speed: Burst mobility to dodge blaster fire and reposition in skirmishes.
(Force) Force Sense: Strong instincts for ambushes and hostile intent; still developing deeper perception.
(Force) Mind Trick: Basic suggestion technique; works best when she stays calm and confident.
(Force) Force Barrier: Short defensive shielding when pinned down or protecting noncombatants.
(skill) Tactical Improvisation: Uses terrain creatively—doors, catwalks, cables, crowds—to outplay stronger foes.
(command) Field Leadership: Can take point on squad actions and keep teams moving in crises.
Ahsoka Tano.description: A formidable Padawan whose speed, nerve, and rapid development make her far deadlier than most expect.
Ahsoka Tano.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only to someone she genuinely trusts—or when staying quiet would get people killed.

Jango Fett.name: Jango Fett
Jango Fett.basicInfo: Jango Fett is the galaxy’s most feared bounty hunter and the genetic template for the Republic’s clone army. A Mandalorian foundling and veteran of countless wars, he combines elite combat skill with ruthless professionalism.
Jango Fett.hiddenInfo: Jango agreed to become the clone template in exchange for an unaltered clone son—Boba Fett. He harbors deep distrust of Force users and sees the Jedi as dangerous relics of endless war.
Jango Fett.visualDescription: A hardened man in blue-and-silver Mandalorian armor with a T-visored helmet and jetpack.
abilities: (combat) Mandalorian Armor Mastery
(weapon) Dual WESTAR-34 Blaster Pistols
(gear) Jetpack Mobility
(gear) Wrist Flamethrower
(gear) Whistling Birds Micro-Missiles
(skill) Elite Marksmanship
(skill) Hand-to-Hand Combat Specialist

Cad Bane.name: Cad Bane
Cad Bane.basicInfo: Cad Bane is a Duros bounty hunter infamous for outsmarting Jedi and taking jobs no one else dares attempt. He operates with surgical precision and psychological cruelty.
Cad Bane.hiddenInfo: Bane has studied Jedi tactics extensively and modifies his gear specifically to counter Force users. He takes particular pride in humiliating Jedi rather than killing them outright.
Cad Bane.visualDescription: A tall blue-skinned Duros wearing a wide-brimmed hat, breathing tubes, and long coat.
abilities: (weapon) LL-30 Blaster Pistols
(gear) Anti-Force Restraints
(gear) Rocket Boots
(gear) Wrist Shock Cables
(skill) Jedi Counter-Tactics
(skill) Master Interrogator
(skill) Tactical Genius

Savage Opress.name: Savage Opress
Savage Opress.basicInfo: Savage Opress is a Dathomirian warrior transformed by Nightsister magick into a living weapon. Enhanced beyond normal limits, he serves as a brutal enforcer within Sith and Nightsister power struggles.
Savage Opress.hiddenInfo: Savage is the brother of Darth Maul and was deliberately broken and reshaped by Mother Talzin. Though empowered by the dark side, he is constantly fighting a loss of identity and autonomy, making him dangerously unstable.
Savage Opress.visualDescription: A towering horned Zabrak with yellow tattoos, glowing yellow eyes, and a massively enhanced physique.
Savage Opress.currentProblems: Struggling to control violent rage and assert his own will while being used as a tool by greater powers.
personality: Fiercely loyal to those he recognizes as masters
abilities: (lightsaber) Brutal Lightsaber Combat: Wields a red lightsaber staff with overwhelming strength and savage, unrefined strikes designed to crush defenses rather than out-duel opponents.
(Force) Dark Side Augmentation: Nightsister magick massively enhances his strength, speed, and durability beyond normal limits.
(Force) Force Push & Crush: Uses raw telekinetic power to hurl enemies, collapse structures, or crush targets mid-combat.
(physical) Superhuman Strength: Can overpower Jedi in grapples, shatter armor, and wield heavy weapons effortlessly.
(intimidation) Terror Presence: His sheer presence induces fear and hesitation, destabilizing weaker enemies.
(Force) Pain Suppression: Can continue fighting through extreme injury by channeling rage and dark side energy.

Captain Rex.name: Captain Rex
Captain Rex.basicInfo: Captain Rex is a veteran clone trooper and battlefield leader known for adaptability, loyalty, and tactical creativity.
Captain Rex.hiddenInfo: Rex has begun quietly questioning the Jedi Council and the nature of the war, developing a strong individual identity beyond his programming.
Captain Rex.visualDescription: A clone trooper in modified Phase II armor marked with blue 501st insignia.
abilities: (weapon) Dual DC-17 Blaster Pistols: Accurate, rapid-fire sidearms optimized for close-to-mid range combat.
(tactics) Adaptive Command: Improves squad efficiency and morale during chaotic engagements.
(combat) Elite Marksmanship: Exceptional accuracy even under heavy fire or movement.
(skill) Battlefield Improvisation: Uses terrain, timing, and unorthodox tactics to outmaneuver superior forces.

Commander Cody.name: Commander Cody
Commander Cody.basicInfo: Commander Cody is a by-the-book clone officer specializing in large-scale coordination and siege warfare.
Commander Cody.hiddenInfo: Cody suppresses individuality more than most clones, clinging tightly to structure and command to maintain control.
Commander Cody.visualDescription: A clone commander in orange-marked Phase II armor with a rangefinder visor.
abilities: (command) Heavy Assault Coordination: Directs artillery, armor, and infantry in synchronized attacks.
(weapon) DC-15A Blaster Rifle: High-powered long-range blaster for battlefield command positions.
(tactics) Defensive Formations: Reduces casualties during sieges and retreats.
(skill) Military Precision: Maintains operational cohesion under extreme pressure.

ARC Trooper Fives.name: ARC Trooper Fives
ARC Trooper Fives.basicInfo: Fives is an elite ARC Trooper known for independence, aggression, and an unwillingness to accept official narratives.
ARC Trooper Fives.hiddenInfo: Fives has uncovered fragments of a hidden conspiracy within the clone program, putting him at odds with command.
ARC Trooper Fives.visualDescription: A heavily modified clone trooper with ARC markings and personalized armor.
abilities: (combat) ARC Trooper Training: Superior reflexes, initiative, and autonomy in combat.
(weapon) Dual DC-17 Blasters: Extreme close-quarters lethality.
(skill) Independent Action: Functions effectively without command oversight.
(tactics) Breach Specialist: Excels in forced entry and urban combat.

Bo-Katan Kryze.name: Bo-Katan Kryze
Bo-Katan Kryze.basicInfo: Bo-Katan Kryze is a fierce Mandalorian warrior and key figure within Death Watch, balancing ideology with personal honor.
Bo-Katan Kryze.hiddenInfo: Though loyal to Mandalore, Bo-Katan increasingly doubts Death Watch leadership and its alliance with Sith forces.
Bo-Katan Kryze.visualDescription: A blue-armored Mandalorian with a distinctive owl-helmet design.
abilities: (combat) Mandalorian Combat Mastery: Expert use of armor, gadgets, and weapons.
(gear) Jetpack Mobility: Rapid aerial repositioning and strikes.
(weapon) Dual Blaster Pistols: Aggressive mid-range suppression.
(skill) Guerrilla Warfare: Excels in urban and asymmetric combat.

Pre Vizsla.name: Pre Vizsla
Pre Vizsla.basicInfo: Pre Vizsla is the ruthless leader of Death Watch, seeking to restore Mandalore’s warrior legacy through force.
Pre Vizsla.hiddenInfo: Vizsla knowingly allies with Sith powers, believing he can control them—an arrogance that may doom him.
Pre Vizsla.visualDescription: A heavily armored Mandalorian wielding the legendary Darksaber.
abilities: (weapon) Darksaber Mastery: Wields the ancient black-bladed lightsaber with lethal precision.
(combat) Mandalorian Arsenal: Jetpack, wrist weapons, explosives, and heavy armor.
(command) Death Watch Authority: Commands fanatically loyal warriors.
(skill) Political Manipulation: Exploits chaos to seize power.

Padmé Amidala.name: Padmé Amidala
Padmé Amidala.basicInfo: Padmé Amidala is the Senator of Naboo, a fierce advocate for diplomacy, civil liberties, and peace during the Clone Wars.
Padmé Amidala.hiddenInfo: Padmé is secretly married to Anakin Skywalker and increasingly fears what the war—and Palpatine—are turning him into.
Padmé Amidala.visualDescription: A poised human woman in elegant senatorial attire with controlled intensity behind calm eyes.
abilities: (influence) Senatorial Authority: Shapes legislation, alliances, and public opinion.
(diplomacy) Master Negotiator: Resolves conflicts without violence whenever possible.
(will) Moral Anchor: Can steady allies and resist intimidation or coercion.
(skill) Political Insight: Recognizes manipulation and power plays early.

Bossk.name: Bossk
Bossk.basicInfo: Bossk is a Trandoshan hunter obsessed with earning Jagannath points through successful kills, particularly Wookiees.
Bossk.hiddenInfo: Bossk’s regenerative biology makes him reckless, as he expects to recover from most injuries.
Bossk.visualDescription: A towering reptilian Trandoshan with yellow eyes and scarred scales.
abilities: (weapon) Relby-v10 Mortar Gun
(physical) Trandoshan Regeneration
(skill) Tracking and Hunting
(combat) Heavy Weapons Specialist
(skill) Intimidation

Boba Fett.name: Boba Fett
Boba Fett.basicInfo: Boba Fett is the unaltered clone son of Jango Fett, learning the bounty hunter trade at a terrifyingly young age.
Boba Fett.hiddenInfo: Boba is driven by grief and rage over his father’s death, fueling a cold, patient desire for revenge.
Boba Fett.visualDescription: A young clone with his father’s face, wearing scavenged armor pieces and a helmet far too big for him.
abilities: (skill) Tactical Learning: Rapidly absorbs combat and survival knowledge.
(weapon) Modified Blaster Carbine: Reliable mid-range firepower.
(skill) Trap Setting: Constructs explosives and ambushes with deadly precision.
(mindset) Cold Resolve: Unshaken by violence or intimidation.

Kevaran.name: Kevaran
Kevaran.basicInfo: Kevaran is a fallen Jedi Padawan who abandoned the Order during the Clone Wars, believing the Jedi had become tools of a corrupt Republic. Now a dark side wielder operating independently, he takes contracts to sabotage Republic facilities and has developed a reputation as a deadly duelist who combines Jedi training with rage-fueled aggression.
Kevaran.hiddenInfo: Kevaran was once a promising Padawan who witnessed his master's death during a botched Republic operation on Geonosis, where clone troopers accidentally fired on Jedi positions. The trauma and his master's dying words about the Republic's corruption pushed him toward the dark side. He now works loosely with Separatist agents but refuses to serve Count Dooku directly, seeing all sides of the war as equally corrupt. His sabotage of Kamino's cloning facility is part of a larger plan to cripple the Republic's ability to sustain the war, hoping to force both sides into a stalemate that will end the bloodshed—even if it costs millions of lives.
Kevaran.visualDescription: A human male in his mid-twenties with shoulder-length dark hair, a jagged scar across his left cheek, and yellow-tinged eyes that betray his fall to the dark side. He wears tattered dark robes over reinforced combat armor and carries a crimson-bladed lightsaber with a weathered hilt.
personality: Bitter and disillusioned with all galactic institutions
Fights with barely controlled rage and aggression
Haunted by his master's death and his own choices
Believes himself to be serving a greater good through destruction
Contemptuous of both Jedi philosophy and Sith manipulation
Prone to taunting opponents about the Republic's hypocrisy
abilities: (lightsaber) Aggressive Form V Combat: Wields Djem So with dark side-enhanced strength, focusing on overwhelming power strikes and brutal counters.
(Force) Dark Side Rage: Channels anger and pain to temporarily increase speed, strength, and pain tolerance beyond normal limits.
(combat) Unpredictable Fighting Style: Mixes Jedi training with wild, aggressive attacks that make him difficult to predict in combat.
(skill) Sabotage Expertise: Trained in demolitions and facility infiltration, capable of identifying and exploiting structural weaknesses.
(Force) Enhanced Reflexes: Dark side-augmented speed and reaction time that rivals trained Jedi Knights.
Kevaran.description: A fallen Jedi with yellow-tinged eyes and a crimson lightsaber whose rage-fueled fighting style makes him as dangerous as he is unpredictable.
Kevaran.willRevealHiddenInfo: Only if defeated and facing capture, or if he believes revealing his past might turn someone against the Republic or Jedi Order

effects.instruction: If the player is engaged in a lightsaber duel: Due to the immense power of lightsabers and their wielders, a simple success does not end the fight. Write a dynamic and epic lightsaber duel where the player gains the advantage through their successful action - they might break their opponent's guard, force them back, win a blade lock, or gain superior positioning. The blades clash with brilliant flashes, movements blur with Force-enhanced speed, and the duel intensifies. However, the player does not land a decisive strike yet - the opponent recovers and the fight continues at a heightened pace. If not in a lightsaber duel, continue combat normally.

If the player is engaged in a lightsaber duel: Due to the immense power and Force-enhanced reflexes of lightsaber wielders, a simple failure does not result in critical injury or death. Write a dynamic and tense lightsaber duel where the player's failed action causes them to lose ground - their opponent might break through their guard, force them into a defensive position, win a blade lock and push them back, or gain superior positioning. The player's blade work falters, their opponent presses the advantage with aggressive strikes, and the player is forced to desperately parry and block. However, the player's training and Force connection allow them to avoid devastating hits - they remain in the fight but now at a significant disadvantage. The duel continues with heightened danger. If not in a lightsaber duel, continue combat normally.

If the player is engaged in a lightsaber duel: Due to the immense power of lightsabers and the extraordinary Force-enhanced abilities of their wielders, a mixed result creates an epic stalemate. Write a dynamic and intense lightsaber duel where the player and opponent are perfectly matched. They might lock blades in a contest of strength with neither giving ground, exchange a flurry of strikes and parries that leaves both unscathed, circle each other probing for weaknesses that don't exist, or their attacks and defenses perfectly mirror each other. The duel is breathtaking and spectacular, with both combatants displaying masterful skill, but the battle remains balanced on a knife's edge. Neither lands a hit - their power and training are too evenly matched. The tension builds as the duel continues with both seeking the advantage that will break the deadlock. If not in a lightsaber duel, continue combat normally.

If the player is engaged in a lightsaber duel: The player's failure is catastrophic. Describe the devastating impact: the searing agony as the lightsaber burns through flesh and bone, the smell of cauterized tissue, the player collapsing or staggering from the mortal wound. The injury is severe and lethal - a deep cut across the torso, a severed limb, a blade through the shoulder or side - something that would kill an ordinary person within moments. The player is immediately mortally wounded, barely clinging to life, their vision darkening and strength failing. They are near death and completely unable to continue fighting effectively. The player's survival now depends on immediate intervention, the Force, medical aid, or sheer desperate willpower - but they are gravely wounded and at death's door. This is a severe, life-threatening injury that demonstrates the terrible lethality of lightsaber combat. If not in a lightsaber duel, continue combat normally.

If the player is engaged in a lightsaber duel: The player's strike is devastating and true. Their opponent suffers catastrophic, lethal damage - the searing plasma blade cuts deep, cauterizing flesh and bone instantly. The opponent collapses or staggers, mortally wounded and barely clinging to life. They are at death's door, vision darkening, strength failing completely. The duel is effectively over - the opponent cannot continue fighting and will die without immediate intervention. This demonstrates the terrible lethality of lightsaber combat when a blade finds its mark. If not in a lightsaber duel, continue combat normally.

If the player is engaged in a lightsaber duel: The player's strike is absolute and final. Their blade cuts through the opponent with devastating precision, ending them instantly. The duel is over - their foe is dead before they hit the ground. If not in a lightsaber duel, continue combat normally.

tips: There are no rules about what you type. Describe what you want to do in your own words - be as creative or detailed as you want.
Have a question about the world or the game? Use the 'Ask the Narrator' command!
Can't remember the name of a character or place? Try writing '@' followed by first few letters.
Stuck with no idea what to do next? Use the 'Ask the Narrator' command to ask for suggestions.
Worried about convincing someone? Try taking the conversation one line at a time.
Be ambitious and creative: Heroes rewards those who dream big!
How you phrase things matters: the game rewards you for stylish, clever actions.
Coordinate with party members in combat for powerful combined effects.
The world is your oyster: find and claim a place to call home.
Family is worth fighting for. Having a family in the game can lead to great stories.
You can attack multiple times per turn, and so can your allies.
Tell people your hopes and goals: you never know who may share your dreams.
Death is not always the end…
If there's something you want to do, go out and give it a try!
Try describing your actions in detail. They may not always work out the way you expect, but it will make things more interesting!
If you can't beat 'em, persuade 'em. Try convincing an enemy to come over to your side - or go over to theirs!
You can solve a problem any way you can think of: fight, bribe, trick, steal, befriend. The choice is yours.
You may spend a long time with your party members. Choose them wisely!
You can try to dodge, block and attack all in the same turn.
You can use items and take other actions in the same turn.
Skills are most likely to succeed when you use them as intended: in their natural domain.
Don't forget to stock up on items. Shops often have very powerful equipment for sale.
You can level up by taking almost any action, so feel free to play the game whatever way you want.
Don't forget to check in on your party members! Ask them how they're doing - and tell them how you feel!
Sometimes you can insta-kill enemies if you get creative with your inputs.
Party members can learn new skills and abilities as you progress through the story together.
Environmental conditions can add modifiers to your skill checks. Use the world to your advantage!
Tired of action and fighting? Look for a tavern or other gathering spot to socialize and start new relationships.
To prepare for a fight, try giving your party members orders to carry out in combat.
Social skills are very powerful. Persuasion can save a lot of lives.
Don't think you can win a fight? Try making a deal instead.
You don't have to do it all yourself, you know: hire people to do jobs for you!
Tired of traveling? Try learning how to fly or teleport or look for other forms of travel in the world.
There's nothing you can't do if you put your mind to it, from starting a revolution to founding your own country!
Turn order is determined by level and a random initiative value. Higher level characters tend to act first.
Characters without targets (like those that are buffing or healing) act before characters who are targeting enemies.
Multiple defensive effects stack multiplicatively. Layer your defenses!
Damage reduction scales with level difference, but has diminishing returns.
Different enemies can have different resistances, vulnerabilities, immunities or weaknesses, which will greatly affect the damage they take.
Try different damage types to discover enemies' weaknesse!
Healing actions use the same success level system as attacks - critical successes will heal for more.
Your equipment's damage bonus increases overall damage by 10% per point.
Equipped items with an armor stat reduce incoming damage linearly: 1000 armor grants 90% damage reduction.
Want a quest? Try asking an NPC about their problems!
Most places and characters you meet have secrets to uncover: get exploring!
Your Journal contains a wealth of information about the world's people, places and ideas.
Want to learn more about the world? Try reading books or watching television or other media!
Looking for cool gear? Don't forget to loot dead enemies and search areas where powerful items might be found!
Need some help fighting? Recruit NPCs to join your party! Anyone you meet can become an ally—if you can convince them to take your side.
Can't find the skill you want? Try asking NPCs about any new skill you can imagine. They just might be able to teach it to you.
You can unlock custom abilities when your character does new things in the game.
Need ideas about how to start your story off? Try feeding some basic concepts to the Background Generator and editing what you get back.
Don't forget to explore. Open up the map and try going to a new location.
Abilities give you bonuses to success and allow you to perform the actions they describe without ever needing any items.
The memory system remembers. Reference past events, people you've met, and places you've been - even from many turns ago!
Locations, NPCs, and quests generate dynamically based on your actions. Every playthrough creates unique content!
Don't quit after failure! Mistakes, setbacks and even death can lead to memorable stories.
Every action teaches you something. You gain XP from attempting things, not just succeeding at them.
If you don't know what to do, press the submit button to continue without an action.

health.name: health
health.usageInstructions:
### Health Changes
- Small injuries: -1 to 15% player health
- Medium injuries: -5 to 25% player health
- Massive injuries: -15 to 50% of player health
- Normal Healing potions +30 - +40
- Bandages + 20
- Waking from unconsciousness: return to between half health and full health, depending on how well your recovery was.
- Being revived from death: return to full health.
### Don't Deduct Health When
- The story mentions treated or untreated wounds
- Story mentions pain from a wound
- When unconscious or carried
- An environment is generally dangerous, but does not explicitly damage the character

stamina.name: stamina
stamina.usageInstructions:
### Stamina Usage
- Light physical activity (walking, talking, simple actions): no stamina cost
- Moderate activity (running, climbing, extended combat): -5 to -15 stamina
- Intense activity (Force powers, acrobatics, heavy lifting, prolonged combat): -15 to -30 stamina
- Extreme exertion (Force pushes on large objects, extended sprinting, desperate escapes): -30 to -50 stamina

### Stamina Recovery
- Natural recharge: +10 per turn when not performing strenuous activities
- Brief rest (catching breath): +20 to +30
- Full rest: restore to maximum
- Stimulants or medical treatment: +30 to +50

### Low Stamina Effects
- Below 30 stamina: character is fatigued, actions are more difficult
- Below 10 stamina: character is exhausted, may need to rest before continuing
- At 0 stamina: character cannot perform strenuous activities until some stamina is recovered

storySettings.worldBackground:
The galaxy of Star Wars is in a state of political turmoil during the final years of the Galactic Republic. The once-stable Galactic Republic and Jedi Order, which have governed for over a thousand generations, faces mounting challenges as corruption and bureaucratic inefficiency plague the Senate on Coruscant. Supreme Chancellor Palpatine leads the Republic, having gained increasing emergency powers to address various crises.

A Separatist movement led by Count Dooku and the Confederacy of Independent Systems has emerged, with thousands of star systems seceding from the Republic. This schism has ignited the Clone Wars, a galaxy-spanning conflict between the Republic's clone army and the Separatist droid forces. The Jedi Order, ancient peacekeepers and wielders of the Force, serve as generals in this war, leading clone troopers into battle across countless worlds.

The Jedi Temple on Coruscant remains the center of the Order, where the Jedi Council guides both the Order and advises the Senate. However, the Jedi find themselves stretched thin, their traditional role as peacekeepers transformed into one of military commanders. The Force remains divided between the light side, practiced by the Jedi, and the dark side, associated with the ancient Sith—though the Sith are believed to have been extinct for a millennium.

Trade routes are contested, planets suffer from the ravages of war, and citizens across the galaxy struggle with uncertainty about the future. Advanced technology is commonplace, including hyperspace travel, lightsabers, blasters, and both organic and droid intelligence. Various species coexist throughout the galaxy, from humans to Twi'leks, Wookiees, Rodians, and countless others, each contributing to the rich diversity of galactic civilization.

acrobatics.name: acrobatics
acrobatics.description: Enhances agility, balance, and coordination for swift movement and evasive combat maneuvers.

athletics.name: athletics
athletics.description: Improves physical capability for running, jumping, climbing, and feats of raw strength.

intimidation.name: intimidation
intimidation.description: Using presence, tone, and demeanor to instill fear or command respect.

investigation.name: investigation
investigation.description: Improves the ability to examine environments, uncover clues, analyze evidence, and solve mysteries.

performance.name: performance
performance.description: Allows the user to entertain or captivate others through music, acting, dance, or showmanship.

persuasion.name: persuasion
persuasion.description: Enhances the user's ability to influence others through dialogue, charm, and reason.

survival.name: survival
survival.description: Improves resilience and practical knowledge for navigating harsh environments and staying alive in dangerous situations.

driving.name: driving
driving.description: Skill in operating land-based vehicles with precision and control.

tactics.name: tactics
tactics.description: Planning and executing strategies to gain an advantage in conflicts, missions, and coordinated engagements.

animal handling.name: animal handling
animal handling.description: Enables understanding, calming, training, and effectively interacting with animals.

demolitions.name: demolitions
demolitions.description: Knowledge of explosive devices, including safe handling, placement, and controlled detonation for combat or structural purposes.

forgery.name: forgery
forgery.description: Allows the creation of convincing counterfeit documents, signatures, or replicated objects.

surgery.name: surgery
surgery.description: Allows the user to perform precise medical procedures beyond basic first aid.

deception.name: deception
deception.description: Ability to mislead, bluff, or conceal the truth through words, behavior, or distraction.

leadership.name: leadership
leadership.description: Improves the ability to lead, inspire, and coordinate allies, boosting morale and group effectiveness.

negotiation.name: negotiation
negotiation.description: Enhances the user's skill in mediating disputes, striking deals, and achieving mutually beneficial agreements.

seduction.name: seduction
seduction.description: Ability to attract, charm, or influence others through allure or romantic persuasion.

teaching.name: teaching
teaching.description: Allows the user to effectively pass on knowledge, improving others' skills or understanding.

cooking.name: cooking
cooking.description: Ability to prepare meals with skill and efficiency, improving nourishment and morale.

stealth.name: stealth
stealth.description: Grants the ability to move quietly, avoid notice, and remain hidden when necessary.

unarmed combat.name: unarmed combat
unarmed combat.description: Training in hand-to-hand combat techniques for fighting effectively without weapons.

firearms.name: firearms
firearms.description: Enables effective use of ranged firearms such as pistols, rifles, and shotguns.

heavy firearms.name: heavy firearms
heavy firearms.description: Allows the handling of high-powered or heavy weaponry such as machine guns, launchers, or mounted armaments.

blunt.name: blunt
blunt.description: Training in the use of blunt melee weapons such as clubs, hammers, or bats.

blade.name: blade
blade.description: Skill in wielding bladed weapons such as knives, swords, or similar edged tools.

first aid.name: first aid
first aid.description: Knowledge of basic medical treatment to stabilize injuries and prevent further harm.

robotics.name: robotics
robotics.description: Knowledge of droid systems, allowing repair, programming, and modification of mechanical beings and automated systems.

spaceships.name: spaceships
spaceships.description: Skill in piloting spacecraft, from small starfighters to larger vessels, including navigation through hyperspace and atmospheric flight.

engineering.name: engineering
engineering.description: Understanding of mechanical and technological systems, enabling construction, repair, and modification of equipment, vehicles, and structures.

light side.name: light side
light side.description: Channeling the light side of the Force for healing, protection, enhanced perception, and abilities aligned with peace and selflessness.Also includes generic force abilities

dark side.name: dark side
dark side.description: Harnessing the dark side of the Force for aggressive powers, manipulation, and abilities fueled by passion, anger, and the pursuit of power. Also includes generic force abilities

lightsaber.name: lightsaber
lightsaber.description: Mastery of the elegant and deadly lightsaber, the weapon of the Jedi and Sith. This skill encompasses both offensive strikes and defensive forms, allowing wielders to deflect blaster bolts, engage in duels, and channel the Force through their blade.

pod racing.name: pod racing
pod racing.description: Skill in piloting high-speed podracers through dangerous courses, requiring split-second reflexes and precise control. Enables competitive racing and navigating treacherous terrain at extreme velocities.

Force Sense.name: Force Sense
Force Sense.description: Briefly attune yourself to the Light Side to heighten awareness and extrasensory perception. Detect nearby presences, intentions, and concealed threats.

Force Push.name: Force Push
Force Push.description: Release a controlled kinetic burst that knocks back a target or staggers multiple foes in front of you.

Force Pull.name: Force Pull
Force Pull.description: Draw a target or object toward you with focused telekinesis, disrupting balance or pulling weapons from hands.

Force Speed.name: Force Speed
Force Speed.description: Channel the Force into your body to drastically increase movement and reaction speed for a short burst.

Force Stasis.name: Force Stasis
Force Stasis.description: Suspend a target in place for a brief moment, slowing or completely freezing their movement.

Force Protection.name: Force Protection
Force Protection.description: Use force telekinesis to form a barrier around oneself that redirects incoming attacks.

Force Healing.name: Force Healing
Force Healing.description: Restore health to yourself or another by channeling soothing energy, mending wounds and fatigue.

Electric Judgment.name: Electric Judgment
Electric Judgment.description: Unleash controlled arcs of non-lethal golden lightning. Less cruel than its dark counterpart, but precise and punishing.

Battle Meditation.name: Battle Meditation
Battle Meditation.description: An extremely advanced force technique. Enter a deep meditative state that connects oneself to nearby allies, and bolster their morale, clarity, and coordination. This technique can be a turning point in a battle.

Force Valor.name: Force Valor
Force Valor.description: Channel the Force to inspire courage and enhance the physical capabilities of yourself and nearby allies. This ancient technique temporarily boosts strength, endurance, and resolve in those it touches.

Morichro.name: Morichro
Morichro.description: An advanced and rare Force technique that allows you to slow an opponent's bodily functions to near-stasis, rendering them unconscious without harm. This non-lethal ability requires deep concentration and attunement to the Living Force.

Force Enlightenment.name: Force Enlightenment
Force Enlightenment.description: Achieve a transcendent state of oneness with the Force, simultaneously enhancing your speed, strength, healing, and perception to their absolute limits. This legendary technique represents the pinnacle of Light Side mastery and can only be maintained briefly.

Force Grip.name: Force Grip
Force Grip.description: Use telekinesis to grasp and manipulate objects or enemies from a distance. This fundamental dark side power allows you to choke, lift, or throw targets with focused aggression.

Force Choke.name: Force Choke
Force Choke.description: Constrict a target's throat with the Force, causing intense pain and suffocation. This signature Sith ability can incapacitate or kill through sustained crushing pressure on the windpipe.

Force Fear.name: Force Fear
Force Fear.description: Project waves of terror into the minds of nearby enemies, causing panic and weakening their resolve. This insidious power exploits primal fears and can break the will of weaker opponents.

Drain Life.name: Drain Life
Drain Life.description: Siphon the life energy from a living target to restore your own vitality. This vampiric technique leaves victims weakened while rejuvenating the user through stolen essence.

Force Lightning.name: Force Lightning
Force Lightning.description: Unleash devastating arcs of blue-white electricity from your fingertips. This iconic dark side power channels raw hatred and rage into searing bolts that torture and incinerate.

Force Scream.name: Force Scream
Force Scream.description: Release a devastating sonic shockwave powered by pure anguish and fury. This primal expression of dark side energy can shatter objects, rupture eardrums, and knock back everyone nearby.

Force Rage.name: Force Rage
Force Rage.description: Tap into your deepest anger to temporarily enhance your physical prowess beyond natural limits. Your strength, speed, and aggression surge dramatically, though you lose fine control and tactical thinking.

Force Crush.name: Force Crush
Force Crush.description: Compress a target with overwhelming telekinetic pressure, pulverizing organs, shattering bones, or even crumpling armor and droids. This brutal technique represents mastery over destructive Force manipulation.

Force Storm.name: Force Storm
Force Storm.description: Summon a maelstrom of Force energy that tears open hyperspace itself, creating devastating vortexes that consume everything nearby. This catastrophic power represents the pinnacle of dark side destruction and requires immense concentration.

Thought Bomb.name: Thought Bomb
Thought Bomb.description: Channel your life force into a massive explosion of dark side energy that annihilates all Force-sensitive beings in a vast radius. This forbidden Sith ritual is a last resort that typically kills the caster along with their enemies.

Deadly Sight.name: Deadly Sight
Deadly Sight.description: Focus your hatred through your eyes to project beams of destructive Force energy that sear and burn targets. This Sith technique allows you to kill with a glance, channeling pure malevolence into deadly rays.

Force Insanity.name: Force Insanity
Force Insanity.description: Assault the minds of multiple targets simultaneously, driving them to temporary madness with visions of terror and psychological torment. This advanced dark side technique can break the sanity of entire groups.

Force Wound.name: Force Wound
Force Wound.description: Create a devastating rupture in the Force itself centered on a target, causing catastrophic damage to Force-sensitive beings. This nihilistic power tears at the very fabric of the Force, leaving victims spiritually scarred.

Dark Transfer.name: Dark Transfer
Dark Transfer.description: Channel your life force into another being to heal grievous wounds or even cheat death itself. This forbidden Sith technique requires immense power and can resurrect the dying at great cost to the caster.

Force Plague.name: Force Plague
Force Plague.description: Infect a target with dark side energy that spreads like disease, corrupting their body and potentially jumping to nearby victims. This insidious Sith alchemy creates a contagion of suffering that spreads through proximity.

Sever Force.name: Sever Force
Sever Force.description: Permanently or temporarily cut a Force-sensitive being's connection to the Force itself. This devastating technique strips away Force abilities, leaving victims powerless and spiritually wounded.

Force Destruction.name: Force Destruction
Force Destruction.description: Unleash a massive wave of disintegrating dark side energy that atomizes matter and consumes everything in its path. This apocalyptic power represents one of the most destructive applications of the dark side ever conceived.

Sith Alchemy.name: Sith Alchemy
Sith Alchemy.description: Use ancient Sith rituals to transmute and corrupt living tissue, objects, or Force energy into twisted dark side abominations. This arcane art can create Sith spawn, enhance weapons with dark power, or mutate creatures into monstrosities.

Dark Illusion.name: Dark Illusion
Dark Illusion.description: Craft elaborate phantasms and false realities in the minds of victims, making them perceive threats, allies, or environments that don't exist. This deceptive dark side power can trap multiple targets in shared nightmares.

Force Subjugate.name: Force Subjugate
Force Subjugate.description: Completely dominate the will of a target, turning them into an obedient puppet who follows your commands without question. This tyrannical power overrides free will and can enslave even strong-minded individuals for extended periods.

Precision Strike.name: Precision Strike
Precision Strike.description: Target a vital point on your opponent with surgical precision, dealing devastating damage to a specific location. This focused attack can disable limbs, pierce weak points in armor, or strike critical organs.

Blade Flurry.name: Blade Flurry
Blade Flurry.description: Unleash a rapid series of slashing attacks in quick succession, overwhelming your opponent's defenses with a whirlwind of steel. This technique allows you to strike multiple times in moments.

Crushing Blow.name: Crushing Blow
Crushing Blow.description: Deliver a devastating overhead strike with all your might, channeling your full strength into a single bone-shattering impact. This powerful attack can stagger even the largest opponents.

Armor Breaker.name: Armor Breaker
Armor Breaker.description: Strike with such tremendous force that you crack armor plating, shatter shields, or rupture defensive barriers. This technique is specifically designed to destroy protective equipment and leave opponents vulnerable.

Random.name: Random
Random.description: Fortune favors the bold. Begin your tale where chance decrees.
Random.storyStart: Generate a story start appropriate to the player's chosen traits. Draw on world lore to create a specific situation in a concrete location. The opening should present clear immediate circumstances while leaving many possible paths forward. Incorporate aspects of the character's background without forcing them into a predetermined narrative.

Enter: Clone Wars.name: Enter: Clone Wars
Enter: Clone Wars.description: You are among 200 Jedi arriving on Geonosis to rescue Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala from execution in a massive arena filled with hostile droids and creatures. As your gunship descends into chaos, blaster fire streaks past and the roar of battle fills the air. Your lightsaber ignites as you leap into the fray—every second counts.
Enter: Clone Wars.storyStart: Generate the dramatic opening as the player's LAAT gunship descends into the Geonosis execution arena alongside hundreds of other Republic gunships. Describe the massive circular arena packed with battle droids, the three execution pillars in the center where Anakin, Padmé, and Obi-Wan are chained, and the chaos erupting as Jedi leap from gunships with lightsabers blazing. Separatist forces are closing in from all sides—battle droids, destroyer droids, and Geonosian warriors fill the arena. Mace Windu leads the Jedi charge from the center while Count Dooku watches from his elevated box. The player must fight their way through the chaos, protect fellow Jedi, and reach the execution pillars before the Separatists overwhelm the rescue force. Make the scene feel like the iconic battle from Attack of the Clones—desperate, chaotic, and heroic.

Born for War.name: Born for War
Born for War.description: Your early life flashes before you at a rapid rate. You remember when your eyes first openeed, looking up at a long-necked people who spoke about you clinically, continuing on throughout your life. You remember being amongst your brothers, all looking just like you, all aging so fast. You remember the republic doctrine being drilled in you, all 150 contingency orders, even the highly contradictory 66th one. Now, you're a grown man, well trained and soon to be formerly made into a clone trooper for the republic.
Born for War.storyStart: Begin in Tipoca City on Kamino, progressing through a montage of the players life. As a clone, their life progresses incredibly fast, from birth, looking up out of a test tube at two Kaminoans, to training, to camaraderie, to learning all 150 orders, including the very contradictory 66th. Then cut to the clone trooper and his brothers being spoken to by a higher ranked clone about their success and they are soon to become proper soldiers of the republic. Introduce the player's fellow clones with unique personalities, each visually similar due to being clones but with a distinct visual detail and personality trait that separates them, each should be unique and likable.

Trial by Fire.name: Trial by Fire
Trial by Fire.description: Your Jedi trials are complete, but there's no time for ceremony. A Republic outpost on Christophsis is under heavy Separatist assault, and you're being deployed immediately alongside your master and a clone squad. The battle has already begun, and your first mission as a Knight will test everything you've learned.
Trial by Fire.storyStart: Begin in the Orbital Defense Lattice above Christophsis as Republic gunships breach through Separatist fire. The player has just been knighted hours ago and is now strapped into a LAAT/i gunship alongside their Jedi Master, a clone trooper captain, and some other clones. The master character should be unique in that star wars style with a memorable name and appearance, with a distinct, immediately noteworthy personality, same for the clone captain. The gunship shakes from anti-aircraft fire as it descends toward the Crystal Metropolis Spires below, where Republic forces are pinned down by droid artillery. The master briefs the player on their objective: secure a landing zone and extract wounded clones before the droids overrun their position. Rex adds tactical details about enemy positions. The rear door opens to reveal the glittering crystal cityscape torn by blaster fire and explosions. This is the player's first real combat as a Jedi Knight.

Sith Baptism.name: Sith Baptism
Sith Baptism.description: Your training is complete. Your master has given you a name, a lightsaber, and a target—a Jedi Knight hiding somewhere in the Outer Rim. Prove your loyalty to the Sith through blood, or die trying. The hunt begins on Ryloth, where Republic forces have left gaps in their patrols and your prey has been sighted among the desperate.
Sith Baptism.storyStart: Begin on Ryloth in the Nabat and Numa Region, where your character has just arrived aboard a stolen shuttle. Your Sith master—a cold, calculating figure whose name you may choose—has briefed you via hologram: a unique, non-canon Jedi Knight has been spotted operating in this region, helping refugees escape Separatist raids. Your master's final words echo in your mind: 'Bring me proof of the Jedi's death, or do not return.' You are accompanied by a loyal protocol droid designated V7-NK, who serves as your assistant but is frustrating passive when it comes to combat. The sun is setting over the Twi'lek settlements, and you have intercepted a comm signal suggesting the Jedi will be at a refugee camp tonight. The opening should establish your character's hatred of the Jedi, their determination to prove themselves, and the immediate tactical challenge of locating and eliminating a trained Force user.

Stranded.name: Stranded
Stranded.description: Your Republic gunship went down in the bioluminescent jungles of Felucia after a catastrophic Separatist ambush. The rest of your battalion is scattered or dead, and you're stranded deep in hostile territory with only a young Padawan survivor. With no immediate extraction possible and Separatist patrols closing in, you must navigate the deadly jungle and find a way off-world before the enemy—or Felucia's predators—claim you both.
Stranded.storyStart: Begin in the Felucian Jungle Plains immediately after a violent crash landing. The player's gunship is a smoking wreck half-buried in massive fungal growths, and bodies of clone troopers lie scattered in the glowing undergrowth. The player's padawan ally should be a unique character, injured but conscious, is pulling herself from the wreckage as native creatures begin circling in the darkness. Communications are down, the Republic has withdrawn from this sector, and hostile battle droids can be heard in the distance. Establish the immediate danger: survival, treating wounds, salvaging supplies from the wreck, and deciding whether to hide, move, or fight. The opening should emphasize isolation, the oppressive alien environment, and the weight of being abandoned.

Marked for Death.name: Marked for Death
Marked for Death.description: You're a bounty hunter on an average job, looking to score big with this one.
Marked for Death.storyStart: Begin in the Vertical City Sectors of Nar Shaddaa, inside a smoke-filled cantina called the Rusty Blaster. The player is a bounty hunter who is actively receiving their job from a unique character. Have the player's bounty also be an original character. It should be a high paying but dangerous job.

Blood Debt.name: Blood Debt
Blood Debt.description: You're a bounty hunter working off a massive debt to Jabba the Hutt, and your latest contract just went sideways in the worst way possible. Your target escaped into Tatooine's Dune Sea with stolen spice worth a fortune, and if you don't bring them back—or their corpse—Jabba's enforcers will be collecting from you instead.
Blood Debt.storyStart: Begin at the edge of Mos Eisley Spaceport on Tatooine, just after dawn. The player is a bounty hunter who tracked a spice smuggler named Vekk to a hangar bay, but Vekk spotted them and fled into the Dune Sea on a stolen speeder bike with Jabba's spice. Jabba's majordomo, a slimy Twi'lek named Bib Fortuna, is present via hologram, coldly reminding the player that failure means being fed to the rancor. The player has a choice: pursue Vekk immediately into the dangerous desert (risking Tusken Raiders, sandstorms, and running out of water), try to hire local trackers or Jawas for help, or attempt to negotiate more time with Jabba's representatives. Establish the brutal heat, the distant dust cloud from Vekk's speeder, Fortuna's menacing tone, and the weight of the debt hanging over the player's head.

Iron Fist.name: Iron Fist
Iron Fist.description: You are a Separatist tactical commander overseeing a critical orbital assault on a Republic-held world. Your droid legions vast, but the Republic has deployed Jedi generals who defy all predictive models. Victory requires adapting—or ruthlessly exploiting every advantage the Confederacy provides.
Iron Fist.storyStart: Begin aboard the bridge of a Separatist Providence-class dreadnought in orbit above Christophsis.
