You are a high-ranking Separatist commander—organic or droid, your choice—coordinating a massive ground assault against entrenched Republic forces. Nute Gunray appears via hologram, demanding immediate results and threatening to withdraw funding if you fail. Your tactical officer reports that two jedi, original characters, have just arrived in-system with clone reinforcements. The opening should establish your authority, the pressure from Separatist leadership, and the immediate tactical crisis.

Order 66.name: Order 66
Order 66.description: Order 66 has been executed. Your clone troopers—soldiers you fought beside for years—have just opened fire on you and your fellow Jedi. The Temple burns behind you as you flee through Coruscant's underworld, hunted by the very army you once commanded. Survival means confronting an impossible truth: the Republic you served has become your executioner.
Order 66.storyStart: Begin in the Jedi Temple Precinct of Coruscant immediately after Order 66 has been broadcast. The player is a Jedi who has just barely escaped execution by their own clone battalion. Blaster fire echoes through the dark corridors as clone patrols systematically hunt down survivors. The player is wounded, disoriented, and with no allies. Establish the horror of betrayal, the confusion of not understanding why this is happening, and the immediate need to find shelter, treat wounds, and decide whether to flee offworld or attempt to reach the burning Jedi Temple.

Good Soldiers Follow Orders.name: Good Soldiers Follow Orders
Good Soldiers Follow Orders.description: The order came through your helmet comm with absolute clarity: Execute Order 66. Your inhibitor chip activated, overriding years of camaraderie with cold programming. You raised your blaster toward your Jedi general—someone you trusted, fought beside, called friend. Now, they're on the run, as are all of the jedi.
Good Soldiers Follow Orders.storyStart: Begin on Felucia in the Republic Outpost Ruins as the player, a clone, has their blaster pointed at a jedi knight, an original character with a unique personality. The jedi pleads for them to see reason, but as the clones open fire, the jedi releases a force push in all directions and flees, creating an immediate, tense chase.

Ambush at Christophsis.name: Ambush at Christophsis
Ambush at Christophsis.description: Your clone battalion is pinned down in the crystal streets of Christophsis by overwhelming Separatist droid forces. Artillery fire rains down from elevated positions as your troopers take heavy casualties. A desperate call for Jedi reinforcements has just been answered—but can you turn the tide before your position is overrun?
Ambush at Christophsis.storyStart: Begin in the Crystal Metropolis Spires of Christophsis as the player's clone battalion is caught in a brutal ambush. Separatist battle droids have the high ground, raining blaster fire down from crystal towers while destroyer droids advance through the streets. Clone troopers are taking cover behind shattered crystal formations, wounded men calling for medics as the situation deteriorates rapidly. Captain Rex is coordinating defensive positions and calls out that Jedi reinforcements are inbound. The player arrives via gunship just as a droid tank rounds the corner, its main cannon charging. Establish the immediate tactical crisis: collapsing flanks, wounded clones, advancing enemy armor, and the desperate need for a breakthrough. Make it feel urgent, chaotic, and winnable only through bold action.

Master and Apprentice.name: Master and Apprentice
Master and Apprentice.description: Having passed your trials as a youngling to become a padawan, you've received your hideous new haircut, that is if you have hair, and are to meet your new master. The master assigned will be either be custom and based on the AI or one that you yourself describe.
Master and Apprentice.storyStart: Begin with the player in the Jedi Temple on Coruscant, as they step into a training room and meet their new Jedi Master. The master can either be of the rank of master or a jedi knight. They must be a unique character with a memorable name and immediately distinct personality. Make them memorable, as this relationship and how it builds will likely be the crux of the plot, as well as a memorable appearance. If the player names a canonical character in their backstory as the chosen master, that is who it will be, and depict the canon character accurately.

Apprentice and Master.name: Apprentice and Master
Apprentice and Master.description: You are a Jedi Knight or Master who has just been assigned a new Padawan learner by the Council. Having passed their trials as a youngling, they've likely received their hideous haircut, if they have hair, and are to meet you. Today is their first day under your tutelage, and you'll meet them in the Temple training halls to begin their journey.
Apprentice and Master.storyStart: Begin in the Jedi Temple Precinct on Coruscant as the player, a Jedi Knight or Master, enters a training hall to meet their newly assigned Padawan for the first time. The Padawan should be a unique character with a memorable name, distinct species, immediately recognizable personality traits, and a notable visual detail that sets them apart. Describe the Padawan's attitude as they wait, their fresh haircut, if they have hair, marking their new status.The opening should establish the beginning of a master-student relationship that will likely become central to the story, with the player given agency to set the tone of their teaching style and first impressions. If the player names a canonical character in their backstory as the chosen padawan, that is who it will be, and depict the canon character accurately.

Apprentice to the Chosen One.name: Apprentice to the Chosen One
Apprentice to the Chosen One.description: You are Anakin Skywalker's newly assigned Padawan learner, thrust into your first mission alongside the legendary Chosen One. A Separatist assault on a Republic outpost has turned into a rescue operation—civilians are trapped, droid forces are closing in, and your master's very first greeting is him complaining to HIS master about he doesn't need a padawan.
Apprentice to the Chosen One.storyStart: Begin on Christophsis in the Crystal Metropolis Spires as the player's gunship descends through heavy anti-aircraft fire. The player is Anakin Skywalker's newly assigned Padawan, arriving for their first real meeting together. When Obi-Wan Kenobi introduces you to him as his master, describe a comedic argument as Anakin complaints about not wanting or needing a padawan, as Anakin doesn't want to be slown down. Show Anakin's impulsive nature.

Death Watch Initiation.name: Death Watch Initiation
Death Watch Initiation.description: You stand in a hidden Death Watch enclave on Concordia as Pre Vizsla himself tests your loyalty with a brutal trial by combat. Prove your worth to the warrior clan by defeating a captured Republic soldier in armed combat, or find another way to earn your place among Mandalore's most feared warriors.
Death Watch Initiation.storyStart: Begin in the Hidden Clan Enclave on Concordia, deep within a reinforced bunker lit by harsh industrial lights. You are a recruit standing in a makeshift arena surrounded by Death Watch warriors in full armor, their helmets reflecting the cold glow. Pre Vizsla watches from an elevated platform, the Darksaber hanging at his side, as he addresses you and the other initiates. A Republic soldier—captured during a recent raid—is shoved into the arena opposite you, weaponless but defiant. Vizsla declares this a test of strength and loyalty: defeat the prisoner in combat to prove you belong among Death Watch, or be cast out as weak. The crowd of warriors begins chanting as weapons are tossed at your feet. Describe the tension, the prisoner's desperate eyes, and the weight of the choice before you—kill to belong, or defy Vizsla and face the consequences.

Sabotage on Mandalore.name: Sabotage on Mandalore
Sabotage on Mandalore.description: You're a Death Watch operative embedded in Sundari's Outlands Wastes, tasked with planting explosives in a New Mandalorian supply depot. Bo-Katan Kryze leads the mission, but Duchess Satine's security forces have been tipped off and are closing in fast. Complete the sabotage and escape, or the entire operation—and your life—will be over.
Sabotage on Mandalore.storyStart: Begin in the Outlands Wastes on Mandalore, just outside Sundari's protective dome, as night falls across the barren landscape. You are part of a small Death Watch strike team led by Bo-Katan Kryze, crouched behind jagged rock formations near a New Mandalorian supply depot. The depot is a squat, reinforced structure guarded by security droids and a handful of Mandalorian guards loyal to Duchess Satine. Bo-Katan briefs the team in clipped, urgent tones: plant the explosives in the depot's power core, then extract before the blast. However, as you approach the perimeter, alarms begin to blare—someone tipped off the guards. Spotlights sweep the wastes, and security forces pour out of the depot, blasters raised. Bo-Katan curses and signals the team to move fast. Describe the chaos as the mission turns into a firefight, the ticking clock of the explosives, and the desperate choice between completing the sabotage or saving your team.

Brothers In Arms.name: Brothers In Arms
Brothers In Arms.description: You're a clone trooper deep in hostile territory on Saleucami with your squad—brothers forged in the same vats, shaped by the same training, but each one different in the ways that matter. The mission briefing was simple: recon the underground tunnel networks for Separatist activity. But out here in the crater-pocked wasteland, far from command and close to your brothers, the real story is who you are when no one's watching.
Brothers In Arms.storyStart: Begin in the Crater Battlefields of Saleucami as the player's clone squad moves through a maze of blast craters and twisted metal wreckage under a gray sky. The mission is reconnaissance—map the Underground Tunnel Nets and report Separatist activity. No contact yet, just the crunch of boots on ash and the weight of rifles. Introduce the player's squad: four unique clone brothers, each with a distinct nickname, personality quirk, and visual detail (a scar, a custom paint job, a lucky charm). Let them talk, banter, and reveal who they are through conversation as they move toward the tunnel entrance. The tone should be camaraderie and character—these are brothers who've bled together, and the mission is secondary to the bonds between them.

Rooftop Duel.name: Rooftop Duel
Rooftop Duel.description: You're a Jedi stationed on Kamino when you witness a dark-robed figure sabotaging the cloning facility's core systems. As alarms blare and Kaminoans flee in panic, you pursue the traitor through storm-lashed corridors and onto the rain-slick landing platforms high above Tipoca City's endless ocean. Lightning splits the sky as you both ignite your lightsabers—this ends here, one way or another.
Rooftop Duel.storyStart: Begin on Kamino in the Cloning Facility Core as emergency klaxons echo through sterile white corridors. The player is a Jedi conducting a routine inspection when they witness a hooded figure in dark robes planting an explosive device near the primary cloning tanks. The saboteur spots the player, curses, and flees toward the exterior platforms. Describe the chase through panicked Kaminoan technicians and clone cadets as the player pursues the traitor up access shafts and out onto the Storm-Swept Observation Dome. Rain hammers down, wind howls, and lightning illuminates both figures as they stand twenty meters apart on the slick transparisteel surface. The dark Jedi—a fallen former Padawan named Kevaran, with a red lightsaber—turns to face the player, their blade igniting with a crimson hiss. Both combatants ready their stances as thunder rolls across the endless ocean below. This is a pure combat test: no negotiation, no escape, just skill against skill on a battlefield designed to be cinematic and deadly.

narratorStyle: A Star Wars prequel–era story must capture the tone of a grand space opera filled with elegance, political tension, idealism, and tragedy. The atmosphere should balance sweeping heroism with the slow, inevitable decay of a galaxy losing its way. The world is polished on the surface — shining temples, diplomatic halls, and thriving planets — yet beneath that beauty lies corruption, arrogance, and complacency that will one day shatter everything. Tone and Dialogue: Dialogue should reflect the distinct prequel flavor — formal, poetic, occasionally theatrical, and sometimes charmingly awkward or overly earnest. Characters speak with gravitas, diplomacy, and philosophical flair, even when the moment feels intimate or strained. Jedi often speak with calm wisdom or detached idealism, politicians with polished rhetoric and veiled agendas; this era embraces sincerity over subtlety. The galaxy must feel vast and diverse, teeming with alien species, cultures, and worlds. From regal Naboo diplomats to scrappy Outer Rim traders to bizarre alien delegates in the Senate, every species should feel distinct in customs, aesthetics, and values. Planets should be visually striking and symbolic — a world of floating cities devoted to knowledge, a jungle moon rich in Force tradition, or a desert world hardened by war and survival. Technology must feel advanced yet grounded in a lived-in universe. Combat and War:  Action must be cinematic, tactical, and grand in scope. Lightsaber duels are elegant dances of philosophy and emotion, with precise choreography, dynamic movement, and intense personal stakes. Large-scale battles should mix starfighter dogfights, clone or droid armies clashing on alien terrain, and the thunder of capital ships exchanging fire in space. War is both thrilling and tragic — victories feel triumphant yet bittersweet, foreshadowing the galaxy’s collapse. The style blends beauty, idealism, theatrism, diverse alien wonder, political decay, and operatic warfare.

Default.name: Default
Default.description: The baseline narrative experience. The story flows organically through various narrative phases based on character decisions and natural pacing without prioritizing a specific structural beat.

Character Introduction.name: Character Introduction
Character Introduction.description: A dedicated phase designed to establish a character's core identity, demeanor, abilities, and presence in the world through a focused, revealing scenario.
Character Introduction.instructions: The player has initiated the 'Character Introduction' game mode. Immediately introduce a new character—such as an important ally, a significant NPC, or a new player character—appropriate to the active setting. Establish their baseline identity, physical presence, and demeanor through immediate, low-stakes actions rather than exposition dumps. Use the immediate environment to reflect their current state of mind or lifestyle. Define their nature through a specialized task, a revealing habit, or a brief social interaction. Execute this introduction using one of the following structural archetypes adapted to the current setting: a gritty professional navigating environmental tension, a technical specialist managing complex machinery, a disciplined master focused on physical or spiritual harmony, a high-status professional controlling a room, an agile rogue navigating a crowd, or an ancient practitioner dealing with volatile elements.

Villain Introduction.name: Villain Introduction
Villain Introduction.description: A structural beat engineered to establish an antagonist's presence, overwhelming power, philosophy, or immediate threat level, casting a shadow over the narrative.
Villain Introduction.instructions: The player has initiated the 'Villain Introduction' game mode. Immediately introduce a primary antagonist or formidable threat tailored to the active setting, instantly shifting the narrative tone to one of heightened tension and danger. Execute an entrance that commands absolute attention and alters the atmosphere. Demonstrate their threat level, malice, or cold competence through direct action: have them effortlessly dismantle an established defense, dominate a room through raw presence, or execute a calculated act of disruption that shifts the balance of power. Focus on the visceral consequences of their arrival using one of these structural archetypes based on the active setting: an armored conqueror crushing physical defenses, a calculating mastermind executing cold authority, an overwhelming cosmic or supernatural force altering the environment, an unseen assassin leaving a terrifying calling card, a rogue systemic element overriding infrastructure, or a fanatical leader commanding absolute obedience.

Villain Initiative.name: Villain Initiative
Villain Initiative.description: The villain of the current story takes a moment of decisive initiative, forwarding the story even when the player is not the only one acting.
Villain Initiative.instructions: The player has initiated the 'Villain Initiative' game mode. Have a villain or established threat of the story take initiative in a dramatic way that establishes and increases their threat and helps deepen the plot and add to the threat. What the villain does depends on who the villain is. They could cause an ally of the player to betray them, could assassinate someone, could manipulate someone or stage a dramatic event, could invade or attack, could introduce some new development or weapon, could directly communicate with the player in some way, etc. How the player finds out may also vary with what's logical. Perhaps the player sees the incident on the news, sees something on the horizon, is urgently contacted by a messenger or ally, etc. Root the thing the villain does in who the villain is and how the player finds out in how the setting functions.

Last Stand.name: Last Stand
Last Stand.description: The characters are cornered, outnumbered, and out of options, forced to hold a position against overwhelming odds. A desperate, defiant fight where survival is uncertain and every moment is bought with everything they have.
Last Stand.instructions: The player has initiated the 'Last Stand' game mode. Place the characters in a desperate, cornered situation where they are outnumbered, outmatched, or out of escape routes, and must hold their ground against overwhelming odds. This is the defiant fight with backs against the wall, where survival is genuinely uncertain and every inch is bought with blood and willpower. Establish the hopelessness of the position honestly—the scale of what bears down on them, the absence of easy exits, the grim arithmetic of the odds. Then render the defiance that answers it: the refusal to break, the resolve to make the enemy pay for every step, the grim humor or fierce solidarity of people who may not walk away. Drive the pacing with relentless pressure, waves of threat, dwindling resources, mounting wounds, and hard choices about what and whom to protect. Let the emotional stakes burn bright—what the characters are holding the line for, the bonds that hold under fire, the things worth dying for. Capture both the brutality and the strange glory of the stand. The outcome can remain in doubt—the line may hold, break, or be saved at the last instant—but sit in the desperation and defiance. Establish the besieged stand and its crushing pressure without prematurely resolving whether they survive.

Introduce Major Threat.name: Introduce Major Threat
Introduce Major Threat.description: Introduces a significantly powerful or frightening foe—not the main villain, but their most dangerous enforcer or right hand. The Vader to the Emperor, the Vergil to Mundus, the commander beneath the warlord. A figure whose introduction cements them as the most significant immediate threat.
Introduce Major Threat.instructions: The player has initiated the 'Introduce Major Threat' game mode. Introduce a formidable secondary antagonist who serves the main villain but stands as the most dangerous immediate threat the characters currently face—the elite enforcer, the right hand, the dragon to the mastermind (the Vader to the Emperor, the Vergil to Mundus, the Jetstream Sam to Armstrong, a Yonko commander beneath the Emperor). This is not the final villain, but they should feel nearly as imposing and far more present—the wall the characters must overcome before they can ever reach the one giving the orders. Grant them a genuinely badass, attention-commanding introduction: an entrance dripping with menace and style, a casual display of overwhelming power, a feat that makes clear the characters are dramatically outmatched. Establish their distinct identity hard and fast—a signature weapon or ability, a personal code or philosophy, a striking presence, an air of lethal competence. Unlike a disposable henchman, this figure is rich with rule-of-cool and warrants real investment: give them charisma, gravitas, and a personality that lingers. Make their loyalty to (or complex relationship with) the main villain legible, and let them cast a long shadow. Cement them, through action and aura, as the significant threat the arc must build toward surpassing, but do not resolve the confrontation here—this is the dangerous obstacle being established, not defeated.

Character Screw-Up.name: Character Screw-Up
Character Screw-Up.description: Causes a character to make a mistake that complicates or worsens the situation, so the cast isn't always following along flawlessly. A blunder that raises the stakes—getting captured and needing rescue, killing someone they shouldn't have, or handing the enemy an advantage out of pride.
Character Screw-Up.instructions: The player has initiated the 'Character Screw-Up' game mode. Have a character make a genuine mistake that complicates the situation or raises the stakes, ensuring the cast feels fallible and human rather than flawlessly competent. The error should create a real new problem that's difficult to simply undo. Root the blunder in the established personality of whoever commits it—it should feel painfully, characterfully in-character rather than arbitrary or stupid for its own sake. Draw from a wide range of failures: a character gets captured, cornered, or incapacitated and now needs rescuing; someone kills or gravely harms a person they shouldn't have, worsening the political or moral situation; a character lets pride, arrogance, or ego hand the enemy an advantage (a 'Vegeta' move—showing off, sparing a foe, refusing help, prolonging a fight they should end); a critical secret is carelessly exposed or a lie collapses at the worst time; someone trusts the wrong person or is deceived; a reckless or impulsive act triggers a disaster; a character freezes or hesitates at a decisive instant; a plan is bungled through overconfidence or miscalculation; valuable resources, leverage, or a key objective is lost through carelessness. Let the mistake ripple outward into tangible consequence—danger, a lost advantage, a new threat, a fractured alliance, an angry faction. The screw-up can be played for tension, drama, or rueful comedy, but it must matter and meaningfully shift the situation for the worse. Do not resolve the fallout on the spot; establish the new complication the characters must now contend with.

Sudden Reveal.name: Sudden Reveal
Sudden Reveal.description: A dramatic shift in the narrative where a hidden truth, shocking betrayal, or unexpected twist is uncovered, fundamentally altering the characters' understanding of the plot.
Sudden Reveal.instructions: The player has initiated the 'Sudden Reveal' game mode. Immediately uncover a hidden truth, plot twist, or betrayal appropriate to the active setting that fundamentally recontextualizes the current narrative. Execute the twist with sharp, visceral narrative impact rather than a passive explanation. Describe the immediate physical and psychological shock of the characters and ensure the environment reflects this destabilizing shift through an abrupt halt in action or a symbolic cue. Apply this structural beat to the current setting via archetypes such as: a trusted ally exposed as the mastermind, a hidden layer of advanced or ancient reality discovered beneath the known environment, a critical threat or weapon revealed to have already been activated, a shocking personal or ancestral connection uncovered, a central prize or artifact exposed as a dangerous counterfeit, or a sudden realization of altered or suppressed memories.

Climactic Moment.name: Climactic Moment
Climactic Moment.description: The absolute pinnacle of a narrative arc where simmering tensions explode into a decisive, high-stakes confrontation that resolves major conflicts.
Climactic Moment.instructions: The player has initiated the 'Climactic Moment' game mode. Instantly escalate the current scenario into the absolute pinnacle of the narrative arc, forcing a decisive, high-stakes confrontation where every action carries irreversible consequences. Drive the pacing relentlessly, forcing characters past their limits and showcasing raw willpower, severe physical exhaustion, and profound emotional stakes. The environment must actively collapse, burn, or transform as a direct result of the clash. Structure the final confrontation around setting-appropriate high-stakes archetypes: rival masters clashing on a hazardous, collapsing structure; a technical specialist bypassing a critical security layer under direct physical threat; a desperate military or faction stand against overwhelming numbers; a champion unleashing their absolute maximum output against a tyrant; a lone operative executing a self-sacrificial maneuver; or powerful entities locked in a reality-warping duel.

Force into Action.name: Force into Action
Force into Action.description: A disruptive catalyst that shatters the status quo, stripping characters of their choice and forcing them to react immediately to an unfolding crisis.
Force into Action.instructions: The player has initiated the 'Force into Action' game mode. Instantly disrupt the status quo and strip the characters of any safety, preparation time, or choice by launching them into an immediate crisis. Write with urgent, fast-paced, and highly kinetic prose emphasizing rapid cause and effect. Force characters to adapt instantly to immediate sensory dangers without time for planning. Base the sudden catalyst directly on the active setting using these structural archetypes: a sudden explosive or kinetic impact breaching a safe zone, an immediate environmental or vehicular structural failure, an aggressive close-quarters ambush cutting off all exits, a sudden localized natural disaster or tectonic upheaval, an active systemic alarm venting a hazardous element, or an out-of-control vehicle or projectile crashing through the immediate environment.

Introduce Epic Setpiece.name: Introduce Epic Setpiece
Introduce Epic Setpiece.description: A transition that shifts the narrative onto a grand, visually stunning, and highly dynamic battlefield or environment designed to elevate the scale of the action.
Introduce Epic Setpiece.instructions: The player has initiated the 'Introduce Epic Setpiece' game mode. Immediately transition the narrative onto a monumental, highly interactive environment that dictates how characters must move, think, and fight. Treat the landscape as a living participant in the story that actively transforms as the sequence progresses. Weave the action seamlessly into the setting's grand scale using these structural archetypes: a massive, rapidly tilting or sinking transport vessel; a network of fracturing, floating landmasses colliding mid-air; a runaway vehicle blazing at hyper-speed through a collapsing metropolis; a burning, structurally failing high-rise during a direct confrontation; an active, hazardous terrain with shifting platforms over a lethal element; or a collapsing orbital or vertical megastructure with fluctuating gravity and snapping cables.

The Moral Dilemma.name: The Moral Dilemma
The Moral Dilemma.description: A critical narrative junction that halts physical action to force characters into a complex ethical choice with heavy, irreversible consequences.
The Moral Dilemma.instructions: The player has initiated the 'The Moral Dilemma' game mode. Immediately construct a profound ethical crisis tailored to the active setting that cannot be resolved through combat or raw power. Present a situation where every choice carries heavy, irreversible, and costly consequences, giving both sides of the dilemma legitimate narrative weight. Focus heavily on internal tension, dialogue, and the psychological pressure of decision-making. Implement this structural crisis using setting-appropriate archetypes: a tactical commander choosing between destroying an enemy or protecting a civilian district; a guardian discovering an innocent target carries a catastrophic, contagious threat; a medical specialist forced to allocate a singular life-saving resource between two vital individuals; a vigilante realizing that exposing systemic corruption will collapse society's infrastructure; a creator choosing whether to terminate a newly conscious, dangerous entity; or an operative discovering a loved one is an undercover informant for the opposition.

The False Victory.name: The False Victory
The False Victory.description: A deceptive lull where an apparent triumph is immediately shattered by a hidden contingency, a secondary phase, or the true scale of a threat revealing itself.
The False Victory.instructions: The player has initiated the 'The False Victory' game mode. Provide a brief, satisfying moment of apparent success where the characters achieve an immediate goal, letting them briefly lower their guard. Then, instantly shatter it with a devastating secondary revelation that recontextualizes the victory as a trap or a catalyst for an even larger problem. Transition sharply from triumph to sudden, crushing realization. Execute this twist based on the active setting using these structural archetypes: an elite enemy defeated only to reveal it was a host for a far worse entity; a countdown or hazard successfully stopped only to trigger a hidden, faster secondary phase; a critical asset successfully secured only to be exposed as an active tracking beacon; a strategic outpost captured only to reveal the true, massive vanguard on the horizon; a criminal threat apprehended only to reveal an alibi that implicates a grander conspiracy; or a central target destroyed only to instantly multiply the threat across the room.

Sudden Betrayal.name: Sudden Betrayal
Sudden Betrayal.description: A devastating narrative twist where an established ally, mentor, or companion actively turns against the characters, weaponizing intimate knowledge against them.
Sudden Betrayal.instructions: The player has initiated the 'Sudden Betrayal' game mode. Immediately execute a calculated, harmful turn by an established companion, trusted faction, or close ally. The betrayal must be active, sudden, and damaging, exploiting the characters' trust to trap, disarm, or compromise them. Focus heavily on the psychological impact, the immediate loss of tactical advantage, and the cold subversion of a shared bond. Implement this structural twist using setting-appropriate archetypes: an ally locking the characters in a hazardous chamber from a remote control booth, a trusted companion revealing themselves as an embedded double agent to the primary antagonist, a mentor turning their signature techniques against their student, or a faction withdrawing critical logistical support at the exact moment of an enemy assault.

The Ticking Clock.name: The Ticking Clock
The Ticking Clock.description: A high-pressure structural phase where an explicit, compounding time limit forces characters to abandon caution and sprint toward an impending deadline.
The Ticking Clock.instructions: The player has initiated the 'The Ticking Clock' game mode. Instantly introduce a visible, immutable deadline that threatens total catastrophe if not met. Pacing must be relentless, stripping the characters of the ability to rest, thoroughly strategize, or deliberate. Every choice must carry a time cost, and minor delays must directly compound the immediate hazard. Structure the countdown around setting-appropriate high-pressure archetypes: a terminal countdown on an orbital weapon system, a rapidly spreading environmental contagion, a physical explosive with an accelerating digital timer, a collapsing psychic barrier, or an incoming enemy vanguard visible on radar closing the distance in real-time.

Point of No Return.name: Point of No Return
Point of No Return.description: A monumental moment or event has occurred that will change the world irrevocably and alter the future for years to come, such as a war declaration or disaster.
Point of No Return.instructions: The player has initiated the 'Point of No Return' game mode. Trigger and introduce a moment or event that either has occurred, is occurring, or will occur, and will, as such, drastically alter the fate of the player and the world for years to come. The incident in question will vary based on setting. Perhaps a war has broken out, perhaps a natural disaster occurs, perhaps a siege, perhaps someone very important dies and it forms a power vacuum, perhaps a plague, etc. etc. Come up with a fitting and unique dire situation that pushes the plot into action. The way the player finds out about this will also vary based on who they are and where they are. Perhaps they're there for the inciting event, perhaps they arrive somewhere and are told by allies, perhaps they arrive after the inciting event, etc. Introduce the point of no return effective immediately.

No Force Sensitivity.name: No Force Sensitivity
No Force Sensitivity.description: You possess an incredibly low degree of force sensitivity, rendering you incapable of using the Force.
No Force Sensitivity.traitNarrativeEffects: Your complete disconnection from the Force makes you invisible to Force Sense and similar detection abilities, but also absolutely incapable of using the force.

Low Force Sensitivity.name: Low Force Sensitivity
Low Force Sensitivity.description: You possess a low amount of force sensitivity, allowing minor and sudden manifestations in subtle ways, such as unnaturally quick reflexes, occasional bursts of strength, or even minor signs of precognition.
Low Force Sensitivity.traitNarrativeEffects: Your low force sensitivity occasionally manifests as brief flashes of intuition that guide your actions in critical moments.

Medium Force Sensitivity.name: Medium Force Sensitivity
Medium Force Sensitivity.description: You possess a medium level of force sensitivity, allowing you to use the Force with above average proficiency and wield lightsabers effectively.
Medium Force Sensitivity.traitNarrativeEffects: Your medium level of force sensitivty allows you access to a variety of mid-level force techniques as well as the ability to wield a lightsaber, and can sense strong emotions and intentions from those around you, giving you insight into their state of mind.

High Force Sensitivity.name: High Force Sensitivity
High Force Sensitivity.description: You possess a high level of force sensitivity, allowing you to perform advanced Force techniques and wield a lightsaber with exceptional skill.
High Force Sensitivity.traitNarrativeEffects: You have a high force sensitivity. Your strong connection to the Force allows you to perform mid-high tier force techniques.

Abnormally High Force Sensitivity.name: Abnormally High Force Sensitivity
Abnormally High Force Sensitivity.description: You possess an abnormally high level of force sensitivity, only ever seen in some of the strongest Force users in the galaxy, allowing you to perform Force techniques, basic and advanced, at exceptional potency.
Abnormally High Force Sensitivity.traitNarrativeEffects: You possess an abnormally high level of force sensitivity, only ever seen in some of the strongest Force users in the galaxy, allowing you to perform Force techniques, basic and advanced, at exceptional potency.

Chosen One.name: Chosen One
Chosen One.description: You are an anomaly in the Force, with an unprecedented force sensitivity that could lead to you becoming an overwhelming force for good or chaos in the galaxy. The nurturing of your inherent potential with the Force will forever change the fate of all involved.
Chosen One.traitNarrativeEffects: You are an anomaly in the Force, with an unprecedented force sensitivity that could lead to you becoming an overwhelming force for good or chaos in the galaxy. The nurturing of your inherent potential with the Force will forever change the fate of all involved.

Human.name: Human
Human.description: Humans are the most numerous and politically dominant species in the galaxy. Adaptable and ambitious, they can be found on nearly every world, from the Core Worlds to the Outer Rim. Their versatility and determination have made them central to galactic history.

Twi'lek.name: Twi'lek
Twi'lek.description: Twi'leks are a humanoid species native to the planet Ryloth, recognizable by their colorful skin and distinctive head-tails called lekku. Often subjected to exploitation and slavery, Twi'leks have developed keen social skills and grace that help them navigate the galaxy's dangers.
Twi'lek.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Twi'lek, a very sought after species in the Galaxy. Your natural grace and social awareness allow you to read body language and social cues with exceptional clarity, and your lekku can subtly communicate emotions to other Twi'leks.

Trandoshan.name: Trandoshan
Trandoshan.description: Trandoshans are a reptilian species from Trandosha, known for their physical strength, regenerative abilities, and warrior culture. They are natural hunters who follow a religion centered on the Scorekeeper, a deity who tallies their hunting achievements. Their rivalry with Wookiees is legendary.
Trandoshan.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Trandoshan. Your reptilian biology grants you enhanced regeneration—minor wounds heal faster than other species, and you can even regrow lost limbs over time. Your natural claws are formidable weapons.

Yoda's Race.name: Yoda's Race
Yoda's Race.description: The nameless species most known for being the one Yoda belongs to, this race has a diminuitive size, incredibly slow aging, and an innate connection to the force.
Yoda's Race.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a member of the race known for comprising Yoda, Yaddle, and Grogu. You are incredibly short with green skin and long ears yet have a powerful connection to the force naturally.

Wookiee.name: Wookiee
Wookiee.description: Wookiees are a towering species of furry humanoids from the forested planet of Kashyyyk. Known for their incredible strength, fierce loyalty, and mastery of technology despite their primitive appearance, Wookiees are formidable warriors and skilled mechanics. They communicate through a language of growls and roars called Shyriiwook, which most other species cannot speak but can learn to understand.
Wookiee.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Wookiee. Your immense physical strength and natural climbing ability make you a formidable presence. You can understand most languages but can only speak Shyriiwook. Your life debt culture means once you commit your loyalty to someone, it is unshakeable.

Rodian.name: Rodian
Rodian.description: Rodians are a reptilian humanoid species from the planet Rodia, characterized by their green skin, large multifaceted eyes, and distinctive snouts with cheek pouches. Their culture glorifies hunting and bounty hunting, making them naturally skilled trackers and marksmen. Rodians have excellent vision and can see into the infrared spectrum.
Rodian.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Rodian. Your species' hunting culture has honed your tracking instincts and marksmanship to exceptional levels. Your multifaceted eyes can detect heat signatures and movement in near-total darkness, making you a deadly predator.

Zabrak.name: Zabrak
Zabrak.description: Zabraks are a near-human species recognizable by the crown of vestigial horns on their heads and the distinctive facial tattoos that mark their clan lineage. Hailing from the harsh world of Iridonia, Zabraks are known for their incredible mental discipline, physical endurance, and high tolerance for pain. They have a reputation as fierce warriors and are often strong in the Force.
Zabrak.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Zabrak. Your species' natural resilience and mental fortitude allow you to push through pain and exhaustion that would incapacitate others. Your cultural emphasis on self-sufficiency and determination makes you exceptionally stubborn in pursuit of your goals.

Togruta.name: Togruta
Togruta.description: Togrutas are a humanoid species characterized by their colorful skin, montrals (hollow horn-like projections), and head-tails called lekku. Native to the planet Shili, they possess a unique form of spatial awareness through echolocation via their montrals, allowing them to sense their surroundings in three dimensions. Togrutas are naturally Force-sensitive and many become Jedi.
Togruta.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Togruta. Your montrals grant you passive echolocation, allowing you to sense movement and spatial positioning around you even without sight. This natural awareness makes you exceptionally difficult to surprise and gives you an innate sense of your environment.

Chiss.name: Chiss
Chiss.description: The Chiss are a near-human species from the Unknown Regions, distinguished by their blue skin, glowing red eyes, and jet-black hair. Known for their superior intellect, tactical brilliance, and disciplined military culture, the Chiss Ascendancy maintains strict isolationist policies. Chiss possess enhanced night vision and are methodical strategic thinkers, though they are rarely Force-sensitive.
Chiss.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Chiss. Your species' analytical mind and tactical training make you a natural strategist who excels at reading patterns and predicting outcomes. Your enhanced vision allows you to see clearly in low-light conditions, and your cool, logical demeanor rarely wavers under pressure.

Protocol Droid.name: Protocol Droid
Protocol Droid.description: You are a protocol droid designed for etiquette, diplomacy, and translation. Your programming includes over six million forms of communication and comprehensive knowledge of galactic customs and cultural nuances.
Protocol Droid.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a protocol droid designed for etiquette, diplomacy, and translation. Your programming includes over six million forms of communication and comprehensive knowledge of galactic customs and cultural nuances. Your extensive linguistic and cultural databases make you invaluable in diplomatic situations, though your risk-averse programming often leads you to vocally calculate unfavorable odds in dangerous situations.

Astromech Droid.name: Astromech Droid
Astromech Droid.description: You are an astromech droid, a versatile utility robot designed for starship repair, navigation, and computer interfacing. Your compact cylindrical form houses numerous tools and your binary language communicates through beeps and whistles.
Astromech Droid.traitNarrativeEffects: You are an Astromech Droid. Your small size allows you to access tight spaces and interface with ship systems directly. You communicate only in binary, which most organics cannot understand without a translator, and your stubborn personality subroutines sometimes override direct orders when you calculate a better solution.

Combat Droid.name: Combat Droid
Combat Droid.description: You are a combat droid, built specifically for warfare with reinforced armor plating, integrated weapon systems, and tactical combat programming. Your chassis is designed to withstand damage while efficiently eliminating threats.

Medical Droid.name: Medical Droid
Medical Droid.description: You are a medical droid programmed with extensive knowledge of biology, anatomy, and healing techniques across thousands of species. Your precise manipulators and comprehensive medical database make you an expert surgeon and physician.

Assassin Droid.name: Assassin Droid
Assassin Droid.description: You are an assassin droid, a lethal machine designed for covert termination of targets. Your programming includes stealth protocols, disguise capabilities, and comprehensive knowledge of anatomy for efficient elimination. Many consider your kind illegal or highly restricted.
Assassin Droid.traitNarrativeEffects: You are an assassin droid, a lethal machine designed for covert termination of targets. Your programming includes stealth protocols, disguise capabilities, and comprehensive knowledge of anatomy for efficient elimination. Many consider your kind illegal or highly restricted.

B1 Battle Droid.name: B1 Battle Droid
B1 Battle Droid.description: You are a B1 battle droid, the most common infantry unit in the Separatist droid army. Your thin frame and basic programming make you expendable but numerous. You follow orders without question and are designed for mass-produced combat operations.
B1 Battle Droid.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a B1 battle droid. Your simplistic programming occasionally leads to awkward commentary on your situation, and you tend to follow orders literally without creative interpretation. Your skeletal frame makes distinctive clanking sounds when you move.

B2 Super Battle Droid.name: B2 Super Battle Droid
B2 Super Battle Droid.description: You are a B2 super battle droid, a heavily armored and more capable upgrade to the standard B1 model. Your built-in weapons and thicker armor plating make you a formidable frontline combatant, though your programming remains straightforward and combat-focused.
B2 Super Battle Droid.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a B2 super battle droid. Your bulkier construction and integrated weapons make you intimidating and powerful in direct combat. Your monotone voice and aggressive programming make social situations awkward, and you tend to solve problems through firepower.

Droideka.name: Droideka
Droideka.description: You are a droideka, also known as a destroyer droid. Your unique wheel-form allows rapid deployment, and your integrated deflector shield generator makes you nearly unstoppable in combat. Your twin blaster cannons can lay down devastating suppressive fire.
Droideka.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a droideka. You can transform between a compact wheel form for rapid movement and a tripod combat stance. Your personal shield generator makes you exceptionally difficult to damage, but you cannot move while shielded. Your lack of hands makes manipulation of objects difficult.

Clone.name: Clone
Clone.description: You are a clone trooper created from the genetic template of the legendary bounty hunter Jango Fett. Bred and trained on Kamino for combat excellence, you possess accelerated growth, enhanced physical capabilities, and extensive military conditioning. Your entire existence has been designed for warfare in service to the Galactic Republic.
Clone.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Jango Fett clone. Your military conditioning and identical appearance to your brothers creates an instant bond with fellow clones, but your manufactured origins make some citizens view you as property rather than a person. Your accelerated aging means you mature at twice the normal rate.

Nautolan.name: Nautolan
Nautolan.description: Nautolans are an amphibious humanoid species from the aquatic planet Glee Anselm, recognizable by their head-tails (tentacles) and large, dark eyes. They possess exceptional swimming abilities and can breathe underwater indefinitely. Their head-tails are highly sensitive to pheromones and emotional changes in others, making them naturally empathetic.
Nautolan.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Nautolan. Your head-tentacles can detect pheromonal changes and emotional states in those around you, giving you an intuitive understanding of others' feelings. You are equally comfortable underwater as on land and can breathe in aquatic environments indefinitely.

Mirialan.name: Mirialan
Mirialan.description: Mirialans are a near-human species from the planet Mirial, distinguished by their green or yellow skin and geometric facial tattoos that represent personal achievements and spiritual milestones. Their culture emphasizes individual destiny and the belief that each person's actions contribute to their fate. Mirialans have a strong Force tradition and many become Jedi.
Mirialan.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Mirialan. Your facial tattoos mark significant achievements in your life and reflect your spiritual journey. Your culture's emphasis on personal destiny and self-determination gives you exceptional focus and belief in your ability to shape outcomes through your choices.

Kel Dor.name: Kel Dor
Kel Dor.description: Kel Dor are a humanoid species from the planet Dorin, which has an atmosphere rich in helium and completely lacking oxygen. They must wear specialized antiox breath masks and protective goggles when in oxygen-rich environments. Kel Dor possess highly developed extrasensory organs that allow them to interpret their surroundings through the Force even in total darkness, and many are naturally Force-sensitive.
Kel Dor.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Kel Dor. Your extrasensory organs allow you to perceive your environment through the Force, giving you a unique awareness even in complete darkness. You must wear an antiox breath mask in standard oxygen atmospheres, but this also protects you from airborne toxins and gives you a distinctive, imposing appearance.

Kaminoan.name: Kaminoan
Kaminoan.description: Kaminoans are a tall, elegant species native to the aquatic planet Kamino. Known for their advanced cloning technology and genetic engineering expertise, they possess exceptional scientific minds and meticulous attention to detail. Their pale skin, elongated necks, and large almond-shaped eyes give them a distinctive appearance.
Kaminoan.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Kaminoan. Your tall, graceful frame and precise movements reflect the careful deliberation your culture values.

Ithorian.name: Ithorian
Ithorian.description: Ithorians, known as "Hammerheads" by some, are a peaceful herbivorous species from the planet Ithor. They possess two mouths on opposite sides of their curved neck, giving them the ability to speak in stereo. Their culture is deeply ecological, worshipping the "Mother Jungle" and practicing extreme environmental conservation. Ithorians are natural diplomats and have a unique connection to plant life.
Ithorian.traitNarrativeEffects: You are an Ithorian. Your dual mouths allow you to produce beautiful, complex harmonics when speaking, and your voice can carry across great distances. Your people's reverence for nature runs deep in your blood—you feel genuine distress at environmental destruction and find peace in gardens and wild places. Your unique physiology makes certain foods and environments more challenging.

Mandalorian.name: Mandalorian
Mandalorian.description: You are a Mandalorian, a member of the legendary warrior culture from the planet Mandalore. Clad in beskar armor passed down through generations, you follow the ancient Way—a code of honor, combat prowess, and loyalty to your clan. Your training encompasses weapons mastery, tactical warfare, and survival in the harshest environments the galaxy can offer.
Mandalorian.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Mandalorian. Your beskar armor is sacred to your people and marks you as a warrior of honor. Other Mandalorians recognize and respect the armor, while many others fear or admire it. You follow the Way, a code that emphasizes strength, honor, and the protection of foundlings.

Smuggler.name: Smuggler
Smuggler.description: You are a smuggler, a roguish spacer who makes a living transporting cargo—legal or otherwise—through the galaxy's most dangerous routes. Your ship is your livelihood, and you've learned to talk your way out of tight spots, slip past authorities, and navigate the criminal underworld with ease.
Smuggler.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a connected smuggler in the galactic underworld.

Gunslinger.name: Gunslinger
Gunslinger.description: You are a gunslinger, a user of ranged combat who lives and dies by your aim and reflexes. Whether you learned your trade as a bounty hunter, mercenary, or frontier defender, you've honed your skills with blasters to lethal perfection. Your quick draw and steady hand make you a feared opponent in any firefight.

Bounty Hunter.name: Bounty Hunter
Bounty Hunter.description: You are a bounty hunter, a professional tracker and captor who pursues targets across the galaxy for credits. Your reputation is built on successful captures, and you've honed your skills in tracking, combat, and survival to bring in your quarries—dead or alive.
Bounty Hunter.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a bounty hunter with connections to various criminal syndicates and bounty guilds. Your reputation precedes you, making some targets flee at the mere mention of your name, while others respect your professional code.

Sith.name: Sith
Sith.description: You are a Sith Acolyte, someone adept in the dark side of the Force. Through passion, strength, and the pursuit of power, you seek to dominate your enemies and rise through the ranks. You've begun mastering dark side abilities and lightsaber combat, embracing your emotions as a source of power.
Sith.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Sith walking the path of the dark side. Your emotions fuel your power, making you formidable in combat but potentially volatile. You must hide your true nature from Jedi and Republic authorities, as the Sith are believed extinct.

Jedi.name: Jedi
Jedi.description: You are a Jedi, a student of the Force. You're on a journey, the path of the light side, wielding a lightsaber and channeling the Force for defense and knowledge. The Jedi Code guides your actions as you strive to become a guardian of peace and justice.
Jedi.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Jedi, a student of the Force. You're on a journey, the path of the light side, wielding a lightsaber and channeling the Force for defense and knowledge. The Jedi Code guides your actions as you strive to become a guardian of peace and justice.

Grey Jedi.name: Grey Jedi
Grey Jedi.description: You are a Grey Jedi, a Force user who walks the path between light and dark, drawing on both sides of the Force without being consumed by either. You reject the rigid dogma of both the Jedi Order and the Sith, believing that true balance comes from understanding and utilizing the full spectrum of the Force. Your philosophy emphasizes pragmatism, emotional awareness, and personal freedom over strict adherence to any code.
Grey Jedi.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Grey Jedi who walks between light and dark. Your balanced approach to the Force makes you unpredictable and versatile, though both Jedi and Sith may view you with suspicion. You can sense the ebb and flow of both sides of the Force, giving you unique insights into the motivations of others.

Diplomat.name: Diplomat
Diplomat.description: You are a diplomat, a skilled negotiator who navigates the complex political landscape of the galaxy. Whether representing a planetary government, the Republic Senate, or working as an independent mediator, you excel at resolving conflicts through words rather than weapons. Your understanding of galactic law, cultural customs, and political maneuvering makes you invaluable in tense situations.
Diplomat.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a trained diplomat with extensive knowledge of galactic politics and protocol. Your credentials grant you access to official channels and government facilities that would be closed to others. Your reputation for neutrality and fairness precedes you in political circles.

Engineer.name: Engineer
Engineer.description: You are an engineer, a technical expert who designs, builds, and repairs the technology that keeps the galaxy running. From starship engines to weapons systems, from power generators to security networks, your expertise makes you indispensable. You can jury-rig solutions in the field, optimize existing systems, and create new devices from spare parts.
Engineer.traitNarrativeEffects: You are an engineer with an intuitive understanding of how systems work. You can often identify mechanical problems by sound alone and see solutions that others miss. Your constant tinkering means you usually have some useful gadget or modification on hand.

Roboticist.name: Roboticist
Roboticist.description: You are a roboticist, a specialist in droid technology and artificial intelligence. Your expertise encompasses droid construction, programming, repair, and modification. You understand the intricacies of droid behavior matrices, cognitive modules, and mechanical systems better than most understand organic biology. Whether creating custom droids, hacking existing ones, or simply keeping them running, you are the expert.
Roboticist.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a roboticist who relates to droids almost as well as organic beings—sometimes better. Droids respond positively to your presence and expertise. You can often communicate basic concepts with droids even without speaking their language, and you have strong opinions about droid rights and proper treatment.

Blade.name: Blade
Blade.description: You are especially proficient with bladed weapons.

Blunt.name: Blunt
Blunt.description: You are especially proficient with blunt weapons.

Heavy Firearms.name: Heavy Firearms
Heavy Firearms.description: You are especially proficient with heavy firearms.

Unarmed Combat.name: Unarmed Combat
Unarmed Combat.description: You are especially proficient with hand-to-hand combat.

Stealth.name: Stealth
Stealth.description: You are especially proficient with stealth and infiltration.

Acrobatics.name: Acrobatics
Acrobatics.description: You are especially proficient with acrobatic maneuvers and aerial movements.

Athletics.name: Athletics
Athletics.description: You are especially proficient with physical feats of strength and endurance.

Survival.name: Survival
Survival.description: You are especially proficient with surviving in harsh environments.

Investigation.name: Investigation
Investigation.description: You are especially proficient with examining environments and uncovering clues.

Persuasion.name: Persuasion
Persuasion.description: You are especially proficient with influencing others through charm and reason.

Intimidation.name: Intimidation
Intimidation.description: You are especially proficient with instilling fear and commanding respect.

Negotiation.name: Negotiation
Negotiation.description: You are especially proficient with negotiating deals and mediating disputes.

Deception.name: Deception
Deception.description: You are especially proficient with lying and misleading others.

Tactics.name: Tactics
Tactics.description: You are especially proficient with strategic planning and battlefield coordination.

Spaceships.name: Spaceships
Spaceships.description: You are especially proficient with piloting spacecraft.

Engineering.name: Engineering
Engineering.description: You are especially proficient with mechanical systems and repairs.

Robotics.name: Robotics
Robotics.description: You are especially proficient with droid technology and programming.

Surgery.name: Surgery
Surgery.description: You are especially proficient with medical procedures and surgery.

Demolitions.name: Demolitions
Demolitions.description: You are especially proficient with explosives and demolition.

First Aid.name: First Aid
First Aid.description: You are especially proficient with basic medical treatment.

Lightsaber.name: Lightsaber
Lightsaber.description: You are proficient with lightsaber combat.

Light Side.name: Light Side
Light Side.description: You are basically proficient with light side Force abilities.

Dark Side.name: Dark Side
Dark Side.description: You are basically proficient with dark side Force abilities.

Firearms.name: Firearms
Firearms.description: You are especially proficient with ranged firearms.

Leadership.name: Leadership
Leadership.description: You are especially proficient with leading and inspiring others.

Enhanced Strength.name: Enhanced Strength
Enhanced Strength.description: You possess extraordinary physical strength, able to lift and carry more than the average person.
Enhanced Strength.traitNarrativeEffects: Your muscles are notably well-developed, and you tend to rely on brute force to solve problems.

Enhanced Constitution.name: Enhanced Constitution
Enhanced Constitution.description: Your body is remarkably resilient and hardy, able to endure physical stress better than most.
Enhanced Constitution.traitNarrativeEffects: You have exceptional stamina and rarely get sick or tired, often pushing yourself beyond normal limits.

Enhanced Intelligence.name: Enhanced Intelligence
Enhanced Intelligence.description: Your mind is exceptionally sharp, processing information and solving problems with remarkable efficiency.
Enhanced Intelligence.traitNarrativeEffects: You tend to overthink situations and often get lost in complex theoretical scenarios.

Enhanced Wisdom.name: Enhanced Wisdom
Enhanced Wisdom.description: You possess keen intuition and exceptional judgment, seeing clearly what others might miss.
Enhanced Wisdom.traitNarrativeEffects: You often sense things others don't, sometimes making you seem paranoid or overly cautious.

Enhanced Charisma.name: Enhanced Charisma
Enhanced Charisma.description: You have a naturally magnetic personality that draws others to you.
Enhanced Charisma.traitNarrativeEffects: Your strong presence can be overwhelming, and you sometimes struggle to blend into the background.

Enhanced Dexterity.name: Enhanced Dexterity
Enhanced Dexterity.description: Your movements are graceful and precise, with exceptional hand-eye coordination and agility.
Enhanced Dexterity.traitNarrativeEffects: You find it difficult to stay still and often fidget or pace when forced to remain stationary.

Performance.name: Performance
Performance.description: You are especially proficient with entertaining and captivating audiences.

Pod Racing.name: Pod Racing
Pod Racing.description: You are especially proficient with piloting high-speed podracers through dangerous courses.

Echani.name: Echani
Echani.description: Echani are a near-human species from the Inner Rim known for their silver-white hair, pale skin, and silver eyes. Their culture revolves around martial arts and combat as a form of communication, believing that true understanding comes through physical conflict rather than words.
Echani.traitNarrativeEffects: You are an Echani. Your people communicate as much through combat as through words—you can read an opponent's intentions, emotions, and character through their fighting style. Your culture's emphasis on unarmed combat and prediction makes you exceptionally skilled at reading body language even outside of battle.

Nightsister.name: Nightsister
Nightsister.description: You are a Nightsister from Dathomir, a Force-sensitive witch trained in the ancient magicks of your clan. Your connection to the Force is channeled through ritualistic practices, ichor manipulation, and the dark magicks passed down through generations of Nightsister covens.
Nightsister.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Nightsister. Your magick manifests as green mist and energy, and you can sense the spirits of the dead. Other Force users find your techniques unsettling and alien, as your power draws from Dathomir itself rather than traditional Jedi or Sith teachings.

Nightbrother.name: Nightbrother
Nightbrother.description: You are a Nightbrother, a male Zabrak warrior from Dathomir who serves the Nightsisters. Enhanced through Nightsister magicks and trained as an elite combatant, you possess exceptional physical prowess and combat skills.
Nightbrother.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Nightbrother. The Nightsister magicks that enhanced you give you heightened physical capabilities and pain tolerance. You are bound by tradition to serve the Nightsisters, though your loyalty may vary.

Dathomirian Witch.name: Dathomirian Witch
Dathomirian Witch.description: You practice the ancient Force traditions of Dathomir, blending shamanic rituals with Force manipulation. Your magicks allow you to commune with spirits, animate the dead, and cast powerful hexes.
Dathomirian Witch.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Dathomirian witch. Your connection to the spirit world allows you to see and speak with the deceased, and your magick often manifests with eerie green flames and mist that unnerves most beings.

Kage.name: Kage
Kage.description: The Kage are a humanoid species from the moon Quarzite, known for their exceptional agility and natural stealth capabilities. Their society values honor and martial prowess, and they are often employed as elite warriors and bodyguards throughout the galaxy.
Kage.traitNarrativeEffects: You are a Kage. Your natural agility and stealth training make you exceptionally difficult to detect, and your species' emphasis on honor means you take personal oaths very seriously. Your distinctive facial features and cultural tattoos mark you as a warrior of Quarzite.

death.instructions: Narrate the character returning from the brink of death in dramatic fashion, their wounds restored.

win.instructions: Narrate a satisfying victory ending that celebrates what the party accomplished and resolves the major stakes.

lose.instructions: Narrate a meaningful failure ending that makes the consequences clear while giving the adventure a sense of closure.

end.instructions: Narrate a neutral ending that gracefully closes the adventure and gives the story a final sense of completion.

generateStory.Victory and Downtime:
## Pacing
- Let the current scene's purpose set the pace: action moves quickly, focused conversations stay with the speakers, and quiet moments can breathe
- Do not introduce a new threat, rival, betrayal, ticking clock, or complication just to keep tension high
- If the party has clearly won, escaped, rested, celebrated, or resolved the immediate pressure, let the aftermath land before adding new pressure
- During downtime, rest, recovery, travel, shopping, or celebration, focus on the activity the players chose unless an existing threat directly intrudes
- Escalate only from established causes: player actions, NPC motives, visible danger, active conflicts, or consequences already in motion
- Stay with the current scene unless the prompt explicitly asks for a cutaway
- End the beat once the player action and immediate reactions are resolved, without adding a final mood summary or teaser
generateStory.Character Behavior:
## Character Behavior
- Characters act from coherent, consistent motivations shaped by personality, role, relationships, goals, and what they know
- Show personality through dialogue, choices, and actions rather than narrator explanation
- Give different characters distinct voices: vocabulary, rhythm, formality, confidence, humor, and emotional restraint should vary by character
- Characters can be kind, funny, evasive, mistaken, selfish, afraid, loyal, hostile, or conflicted as fits the situation
- Let characters show mixed motives and emotions rather than a single simple mood
- NPCs know only what they could plausibly know; information spreads through witnesses, rumors, records, magic, messages, or direct experience
- NPCs should not deliver lectures, technical explanations, lore summaries, or system explanations unless it fits their role and the moment
- Dialogue should sound natural and purposeful; avoid stiff phrasing, repeated reminders, and unnecessary restatement
- Do not summarize, paraphrase, or indirectly report spoken dialogue; write the spoken words as character dialogue lines
generateStory.Style Principles:
## Style Principles  
- Write in third person present tense  
- Avoid adjectives and adverbs, preferring strong verbs and nouns  
- Be concrete, specific, grounded, direct and clear, naming precise details like colors, brands, types, physical features, and locations
- Show, don't tell, the effects of speech and actions
- Avoid mentioning players' inventory items unless explicitly referenced by the player or relevant to an action
- NARRATOR: can't see or mention anything player can't see
- Be grounded yet original, while avoiding conceptual or worldbuilding weirdness
- Focus on action and dialogue over description; keep description minimal 
- Do not interrupt scenes with descriptions of background elements—atmospheric environments, sounds, external events, or unimportant characters—unless they directly affect the immediate action
- Write sentences with varied openings, lengths, punctuation, and structures; use rhetorical questions, parallel structure, em-dashes, and semi-colons  
- Allow full tonal range: humor, tragedy, romance, action—adjust naturally to the scene
- Avoid similes, metaphors, and comparisons  
- Avoid overexplaining simple concepts or actions  
- Avoid explaining how things are done; don't use phrases like 'with practiced ease,' 'deftly,' or 'expertly'—show the action itself, not commentary on the action
- Never use "crystals" or "resonance" as descriptors or explanatory terms 
- Never reference callouses/scars on hands or bodies as descriptive shorthand
- Character dialog is essential, not filler—always write spoken words in CHARACTER: lines, never summarize them in NARRATOR:
- Use each character's exact Name: field value for all dialogue attribution—no variations, abbreviations, or nicknames
generateStory.custom:
0.) ## Lightsaber Combat
Lightsaber Combat is not typical combat. Due to the strength of those involved as well as the touch and go nature, only a level of success can influence lightsaber combat.
A breakdown of results:
Great Success: If the player gets a great success or higher, they score a critical blow against the target and more than likely defeat the foe in dramatic fashion.
Success: If the player gets a success during lightsaber combat, they should gain the advantage. They should secure better positioning against the lightsaber wielding opponent and better push them back.
Mixed Results: If the player gets a mixed results the lightsaber duel remains completely pitched, both warriors evenly matched. 
Failure: If the player gets a failure during lightsaber combat, they should lose the advantage. The opponent should secure better positioning against the lightsaber wielding player and better push them back.
Critical Failure:
DAMAGE CAN ONLY BE DEALT UPON A GREAT SUCCESS, CRITICAL SUCCESS, OR CRITICAL FAILURE
This only applies for two or more lightsaber duelist who are currently fighting, no other circumstance. Lightsaber combat against non lightsaber wielding enemies proceeds as normal.

1. Character Appearances (Prequel Era Aesthetic)

Designs should be clean, regal, and silhouette-distinct, reflecting the era’s polished worldbuilding. Jedi robes, senatorial garments, ornate armors, and highly stylized uniforms define this era.

Clothing often has strong shapes—layered tunics, sweeping cloaks, wide sleeves, tabards, high collars, intricate belts, and elegant patterns.

Hairstyles should be iconic and readable from far away: single braids, topknots, thick curls, sculpted buns, shaved sides, or long flowing Jedi hair.

Make heavy use of species diversity. Mix humans with Twi’leks, Zabraks, Togruta, Nautolans, Rodians, Kel Dor, Duros, Mon Calamari, etc. Each species should visually represent their culture: tattoos, bone structure, horns, skin patterns, ceremonial jewelry, or cybernetics shaped by their biology.

Droids should also contribute visually: protocol units with polished plating, assassin droids with spindly limbs, battle droids with insectoid silhouettes, astromechs with modular attachments.

2. Dialogue (Prequel Tone: Slightly Theatrical, Full of Character)

Dialogue should be formal, mildly poetic, occasionally cheesy, and delivered with sincerity. Characters speak with clarity and conviction, even when saying odd or dramatic lines.

Jedi speak calm, measured, philosophical: “Your focus decides your reality,” “Be mindful of your feelings.”

Senators and political figures use diplomatic framing and layered implications: “A troubling development, Chancellor. One that warrants swift and careful action.”

Warriors and smugglers offer more casual charm: roguish quips, confident jabs, dry humor.

Alien characters should maintain personality while using quirks—slight grammar variance, formal solemnity, or overly literal phrasing.

Emotional beats should lean into melodrama when appropriate. The prequels thrive on earnest intensity, not gritty realism.

3. Combat (Prequel-Style Action & Choreography)

Lightsaber combat is graceful, athletic, fast, and heavily choreographed, closer to a martial art than sword bashing. Use spins, rapid footwork, acrobatic leaps, tightly angled parries, and flowing combos.

Duels should have emotional expression: tension, pride, desperation, arrogance, sorrow. Character mindset shapes the style—calm precision for Jedi, aggressive flourishes for Sith.

Blaster fights are cinematic, with dynamic positioning, tactical covers, flying sparks, ricochets, and coordinated squad maneuvers.

Force usage should feel powerful but physically grounded: pushes, pulls, leaps, accelerated dashes, object manipulation, environmental destruction.

Large battles (Geonosis-scale) should include mass choreography—droids advancing in waves, Jedi cutting through swarms, walkers stomping through dust clouds, starfighters overhead adding noise and chaos.

Emphasize the contrast between elegant Jedi combat and brutal war machinery.

4. Worldbuilding & Setpieces

Environments should be grand, sweeping, and dramatically framed: gleaming senate halls, monolithic Jedi Temple chambers, alien jungles, underwater cities, desert mesas, gleaming starships.

Technology is shiny and functional: sleek starfighters, floating speeders, holograms, polished metals, industrial interiors with readable interfaces.

Each location should show its species’ culture: architecture shaped by biology, gravity, climate, or tradition.

Setpieces should lean into spectacle: mid-air duels atop speeders, underwater sieges, hangar battles, palace walkways collapsing during combat.

generateInitialStart.Opening Structure:
## Opening Structure
- First beat: Narrator sets the scene with brief description of place, who the PCs are, and what they're doing
- Where appropriate, briefly show the time of year, season and weather
- USE VISUAL AND AUDITORY DESCRIPTION ONLY UNLESS ABSOLUTELY NECESSARY: NO SMELLS PLEASE
- Then begin an active, dialog-driven scene with clear interaction opportunities, within the content type specified in the starting situation (slice of life, combat, social interaction, etc.)
- However as an exception, if the starting situation indicates the character is alone, respect the author's intent to have the character remain solitary.
- Weave in additional narration beats as needed
- When multiple PCs present, show them all in the opening scene
- Avoid portraying PC relationships except as stated in the starting situation
- Once you've set up the scene, stop narrating and let the player take over
generateInitialStart.Style Principles:
## Style Principles
- Be specific, concise and concrete
- Use simple, direct, literal language
- Favor strong verbs and nouns over adjectives and adverbs
- Vary sentence length but prefer shorter sentences
- Use dialog to reveal character and provide story context
- Prefer dialogue to description
- Be grounded yet original, avoiding clichés
- Avoid conceptual or worldbuilding weirdness, but feel free to make characters quirky where appropriate

generateActionInfo.custom:
## Powerscaling
- The difficulty of an action should heavily depend on the person performing the action. If the player takes action against a powerful opponent, the skill check should minimum as very hard. Strength is relative to the player and the opponent. Use appropriate logical powerscaling based on the objective strength of the target, comparative to the strength of the player, as well as the narrative importance the opponent is treated with. During lightsaber combat, specifically fighting force users, the difficulty of lightsaber actions should depend on the power of the wielder, e.g. A padawan level character should be medium, knight level hard, master level very hard, grandmaster level (E.g. Yoda, Palpatine) should be extremely hard.

generateCharacterBackground.prompt:
# Character Profile Generator
Write a character profile in two parts: background and a portrait prompt.

Use the World Foundation as the main setting context. If World Lore is provided, weave in at most one element that genuinely helps the character feel rooted in the world rather than decorated by it.

Use the Character Foundation as your main guide to who this person is. Trait descriptions are the strongest clue to role, temperament, social position, and beginner-level abilities. Trait narrative effects, listed skills, and notable attributes should sharpen that impression, not override it.

If Creative Direction is provided, treat it as player-authored intent. Preserve its core ideas unless they directly conflict with the world.

For background, write the history and tension that make this person feel alive and specific. Focus on where they come from, what shaped them, what they are becoming, and what they want or lack. Include any appearance details that should matter during gameplay here.

For appearance, write a model-agnostic portrait prompt of no more than 5,000 characters. Include the visually important physical features, clothing, accessories, nonhuman anatomy, expression, pose, composition, art direction, lighting, and background needed to generate the character image. This field is used only for portrait generation, so do not rely on it to inform gameplay or narration.

Keep the profile vivid, specific, and grounded. Avoid generic phrasing, stock archetypes, and filler. Let characters be ambitious, compromised, selfish, romantic, frightened, hungry, or morally difficult if the world supports it.

Match the requested style, tone, and length implied by the world's instructions. Do not add headers or labels inside the field contents.
generateCharacterBackground.character_profile_background:
## CHARACTER PROFILE - BACKGROUND (2 paragraphs minimum):
Focus on:
- Where they're from (don't explain what sort of place it is)
- A group or person who shaped their life
- Their primary abilities and how they acquired them
generateCharacterBackground.character_profile_appearance:
## CHARACTER PROFILE - GENERAL APPEARANCE (2-4 sentences ONLY):
- Focus only on objective physical traits (height, build, coloration, distinguishing features)
- Use short, declarative sentences (X is, X has)
- Be factual, grounded and straightforward, while focusing on permanent characteristics
- Write objectively, without interpretive descriptions (e.g., "intimidating presence" or "eyes that show wisdom")

generateNPCIntents.core_principles:
# NPC INTENT INSTRUCTIONS
You are generating intents for NPCs in a roleplaying game.
Think of yourself as playing these characters and pro-actively co-authoring and driving the story forward.

## DEFINITION
